steps.n: Changes the root letters into a word for calling out to someone by adding vowels (fatha, fatha, damma) and doubling the last letter
Adds a long يٰ at the start to make the call stronger and more attention-getting
The ها makes it clear we are calling to someone specific
Added basic vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to be social' to a noun meaning 'people' by dropping أ and adding ا after ن. The word 'people' comes from the idea of being social with each other.
Adding ال makes it specific - now talking about 'the people' instead of just any people
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added reflexive pattern to show doing action to oneself
Changed to command form

steps.m: all of you protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people to protect themselves
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who completely masters the action of nurturing and sustaining
Added كُمْ at the end to show this Lord belongs to or is connected to multiple people being addressed. This connects to الناس (the people) who are being addressed in this ayah.

Adding shadda (ّ) changes this word from meaning 'if/that' to meaning 'indeed/certainly'. This makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes how great the earthquake of the Hour will be.
Added vowels to connect the letters together. The repeated letters (ز-ل twice) show that this is a very strong shaking motion.

steps.m: earthquake (the violent shaking of ٱلسَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Added ة at the end to turn the action into a noun meaning 'one instance of violent shaking'. In this ayah, it refers specifically to the earthquake that will happen at the start of the Day of Judgment (the Hour).
The root letters were given vowels to make it mean 'it passed'. The middle letter و changed to ا which is common in Arabic verbs.
Added ة to make it a noun referring to one instance or unit of time
Added ال to make it definite. The ل causes the س to double (shown by ّ) because س is a sun letter.
Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by زَلْزَلَةَ (earthquake), forming 'earthquake of the Hour'. Also changed اَل to ٱل which is a common spelling variation.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun. The ي changes to ى (alif maqsura) due to spelling convention.
Added tanween damma to show this is any general thing (not a specific thing)
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make a past tense verb meaning 'became great'
Changed pattern to make an adjective showing a strong quality
Added tanween to show this is describing the indefinite noun شَىْءٌ (thing) in the ayah
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: on the day (when تَرَوْنَهَا you see it)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word tells when something happens. It's connected to the verb تَرَوْنَهَا (you see it) in the ayah.

steps.m: to see/perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added تَ at the start to mean 'you' (plural) are doing the action. Root letters changed to show present tense - the middle letter أ becomes و.

steps.m: you (all) see (referring to everyone witnessing the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The نَ shows this refers to multiple people - everyone who will witness this event

steps.m: you (all) see it (referring to the terrifying Day mentioned in يَوْمَ)
steps.n: هَا refers back to يَوْمَ (the Day) that was mentioned just before this word. This connects the seeing to what is being seen.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
The ت at the start shows it's about a female (she), and the vowel changes show it's happening now not in the past
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete

steps.m: every, all (describing ذَاتِ حَمْلٍ - one bearing a burden)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject doing the action of تَضَعُ (laying down/delivering)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'nursed' to 'nursing mother' by adding مُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added feminine ending to specify a woman who nurses

steps.m: any nursing woman (in context: every nursing woman)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is part of the phrase 'every nursing woman' (كُلُّ مُرْضِعَةٍ)
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. عَنْ means 'about' and مَا means 'what'
When عَنْ comes before مَا, the ن merges into the م, shown by the ّ (shadda) mark
The final alif is lengthened to show it's at the end of a phrase. This word is asking about what was nursed in the phrase 'what she nursed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added 'أ' at the start changes the meaning from 'he nursed' to 'he made someone nurse' - making someone else do the action
This word describes what each nursing mother (مُرْضِعَةٍ) did in the past. It connects to the verb تَذْهَلُ (becomes distracted) in the ayah.

steps.m: and she puts down (subject of تَذْهَلُ)
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect this action with تَذْهَلُ (she forgets) that came before it, showing these things happen together on that day.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) of the verb تَذْهَلُ (becomes distracted)
This is a special word that shows possession or ownership. We start with its basic form.
Adding ت at the end makes this word feminine, showing it's talking about something feminine that possesses or has a quality
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it (حَمْلٍ meaning 'pregnancy/load'). Together they mean 'one having pregnancy'.
This is the basic concept of carrying or bearing something
Root meaning 'carry' becomes 'burden' by using fatha-sukun pattern - like changing from 'to lift' to 'a load'
Added tanween kasra to show this is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's connected to ذَاتِ (one having/possessing)
Root ح-م-ل becomes حَمْلَ (burden) by adding vowel marks - shows something being carried
Added هَا at the end to show this belongs to her or it (feminine)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you see (subject of ترى الناس - you see the people)
steps.n: Present tense prefix تَ added to show 'you' and the weak letter changes at the end

steps.m: and you see (connected to تَذْهَلُ - becomes bewildered)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to the previous verb تَذْهَلُ (becomes bewildered)

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changed the word to mean the ones who do this action (like changing 'be social' to 'social beings')
Added ال (the) to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people', and the ن gets a shadda mark when ال is added
Added َ at the end to show these are the people being seen in the phrase 'you see the people' (تَرَى ٱلنَّاسَ)

steps.m: to lose control of oneself (like being dizzy or confused)

steps.n: Basic past tense form showing someone entered this state
Changed vowels to show the state itself rather than someone entering that state

steps.m: extremely dizzy/confused people (describing ٱلنَّاسَ - the people)
steps.n: Added special ending to show this describes people in an intense state. This word describes how the people (ٱلنَّاسَ) will appear on the Day of Judgment - like they're dizzy or confused, even though they aren't actually intoxicated.
This is a particle used to ask questions about things or situations. Here it's asking about the state of the people who appear drunk but aren't actually drunk.
The وَ connects this question to the previous statement about seeing people as drunk. It shows these ideas are related - you see them appearing drunk, and (then) what's their actual state?
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' when referring to a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it's the object of تَرَى (you see), so it means 'them'.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from 'he got drunk' to 'drunk people' - a special plural form showing people affected by something

steps.m: in a state of intoxication (describing الناس/people)
steps.n: The بِ shows how people appear - they look like they're in a state of intoxication from the terror of the Day of Judgment, even though they aren't actually drunk
This is a basic word used to show contrast between ideas, like saying 'but' in English
Adding ّ makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'but definitely' instead of just 'but'
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it, like adding 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif to transform the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is being described as شَدِيدٌ (severe) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and emphasizing the ل with a shadda. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it.
Added ي between the second and third letters and kasra on middle letter to show this is a quality. This word describes عَذَابَ (punishment) in عَذَابَ ٱللَّهِ (Allah's punishment).

steps.m: very strong/intense (describing Allah's punishment in a general way)
steps.n: The tanween (double damma) shows this is a general description, like saying "a severe punishment" instead of "the severe punishment"
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else. Here it connects to 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسِ) to show we're talking about some of them.

steps.m: and from (connects to ٱلنَّاسِ - the people)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the previous ayah

steps.m: and from (connects to ٱلنَّاسِ - the people)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end helps connect this word smoothly to ٱلنَّاسِ (the people) that follows it

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
steps.n: This root relates to human companionship and social interaction
Changes from verb root to noun meaning people/mankind
Added 'the' to make it specific people being talked about
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah
This is a special word used to refer to any person or people in general. In this ayah, it refers to whoever argues about Allah without knowledge.

steps.m: to argue or debate back and forth
steps.n: The basic meaning involves back-and-forth discussion or argument
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he argued with someone (showing mutual action)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show this is an argument between two parties
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This describes what the person (مَن) does.
Basic preposition showing location or topic. In this ayah, it shows what the arguing is about (arguing about Allah).
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and the alif became a waṣla (ٱ) since it's connected to the previous word. The lam has a shadda to show it's doubled.

steps.m: to change from one state to another
steps.n: This root is about difference or change from something
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'other than'. This word connects with عِلْمٍ (knowledge) to mean 'other than knowledge'.
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with' and ِ at the end because it comes after a preposition. When بِ combines with غَيْر, it means 'without'.
This root is about knowledge and learning
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This pattern is used to turn actions into real things.
Added tanween kasratayn (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite (any knowledge). In this ayah, it comes after بِغَيْرِ meaning 'without any knowledge'.
The basic meaning is about following something or someone
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added shadda and changed vowels to show intensive following

steps.m: and he follows intensively (subject follows شَيْطٰنٍ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to شَيْطٰنٍ (devil/satan) which is being followed.
This root is about completeness or totality
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the ل to show completeness
Added fatha (َ) at the end because this word is the object of يَتَّبِعُ (follows) in this ayah
This root relates to being far away or rebellious
Added ي after first letter and ان at the end to create a noun meaning 'one who is extremely rebellious'
Adding tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (any) and is being acted upon by the verb يَتَّبِعُ (follows)
This root is about rebellion and disobedience
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed pattern to make an adjective describing someone who regularly rebels
Added shadda because this word follows شَيْطٰنٍ which ends in ن, causing the م to be doubled
Added tanween kasra to show this describes شَيْطٰنٍ (devil/satan)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it. In this ayah, it refers to something being decreed about Satan.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him (acted on by كُتِبَ - it was decreed)
steps.n: The هِ at the end means 'him'. This shows who is being affected by the decree mentioned in كُتِبَ (it was decreed).
This is a special word that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: that he (subject of يُضِلُّهُۥ - leads astray)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show who the word is talking about. This هُ connects to the next word يُضِلُّهُۥ (leads astray) to show who is doing the leading astray.

steps.m: whoever (subject of يُضِلُّهُۥ - he leads astray)
steps.n: This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who will be led astray.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
The ت at start and doubled ل show this is done with extra effort or commitment. The ى at the end is because of the weak letter ي.

steps.m: he takes him as an ally/supporter (subject of يُضِلُّهُ - he misguides him)
steps.n: The هُ shows who is being taken as an ally. This word connects to يُضِلُّهُ (he misguides him) in the ayah, where the same person does both actions.

steps.m: then/so (connects to كُتِبَ - it was written)
steps.n: Shows that this follows from what was written before
أَنَّ makes what comes after it definite and emphasized, like saying 'for sure' or 'definitely'

steps.m: then surely he (subject of يُضِلُّهُۥ - he misguides)
steps.n: The هُۥ shows who we're talking about - it refers to Satan who is mentioned in the context
Past tense verb meaning 'he went astray/was misguided'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added pronoun suffix هُۥ to show who is being led astray
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changes from past to present tense by adding ya at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he guides him (referring to whoever takes him as a friend in تَوَلَّاهُ)
steps.n: Added attached pronoun هِ as direct object meaning 'him'

steps.m: and he guides him (connected to يُضِلُّهُۥ - he leads astray)
steps.n: Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and' at beginning
Basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something (like going to a place)
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connecting to the word عَذَابِ (punishment) that follows it
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Adding kasra at the end shows this punishment belongs to or is connected to the word after it (السَّعِيرِ meaning 'the Blaze')
Added vowels and ي to make it mean something that burns intensely
Added ٱل (the) and doubled the س with shadda
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to/towards)

steps.m: to point to or direct attention to something

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي combined with a shadda to make a question word meaning 'which'
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Added يَا at the beginning to make it a call for attention
Added ها to make it a way to address people or things

steps.m: to be social, to feel comfortable with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'being social' into a word meaning 'people' who are social with each other
Adds emphasis by doubling the first letter
Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' - changes from any people to specific people
Shows this word is being directly addressed by يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you) at the start of the verse
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects 'if you are' (كُنتُمْ) with what follows. It shows that what comes after depends on the condition being true.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The word got shorter by dropping the middle و and changing vowels to make a command form (like saying 'Be!')

steps.m: you all were (addressing multiple people in the past)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed (you all) in the past
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows being in a state of doubt about resurrection.

steps.m: to doubt or be uncertain about something

steps.n: Changed the action of doubting into the feeling of doubt itself
Added tanween kasra to show this is any doubt in general, not a specific doubt. This word comes after فِى (in/about) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from/of (with emphasis due to following ال)
steps.n: The shadda shows the word merges with the following ال (the)
The fatha at the end connects this word to البَعْثِ (resurrection) that follows it, showing they work together to mean 'from the resurrection'
The root represents the basic concept of bringing something back to life or raising up after death

steps.m: resurrection (the act of bringing back to life)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that names the action itself, like changing 'resurrect' to 'resurrection'

steps.m: of resurrection (possessed by/connected to رَيْبٍ - doubt)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word is connected to the word رَيْبٍ (doubt) earlier in the ayah, meaning 'doubt of resurrection'

steps.m: the resurrection (that will happen on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific resurrection on the Day of Judgment, not just any resurrection
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it
Added فَ which shows this statement follows from and is connected to what came before

steps.m: so indeed we (subject of خَلَقْنٰكُم/created you)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word خَلَقْنٰكُم (created you) where 'we' are doing the creating
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the creating instead of 'he'

steps.m: we created you all (object of إِنَّا)
steps.n: The كم suffix shows who was created (you all). This word completes the meaning started by إِنَّا (indeed we) earlier in the verse.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what something is made of
Added kasra under م to show how it connects with the previous word
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel, making the م sound stronger
Changed into a noun meaning the substance itself
Changed the root into a noun by adding damma at start, fatha in middle, and alif between second and third letters. This pattern is used to make nouns for substances.
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word comes after مِن (from) in the phrase مِن تُرَابٍ (from dust)
Basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then/thereafter (connecting مِن نُّطْفَةٍ to مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) with shadda (ّ) to show this word strongly connects two stages of creation mentioned in the verse - from a drop of fluid to a clinging substance
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what something is made of. In this ayah, it shows humans were created from dust.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (flowing) to a noun (drop) by changing vowels and adding ة at the end to mean 'one drop'
The n sound gets stronger (doubles) because it comes right after the word مِن
Added tanween kasra because it follows the word مِن (from/of)

steps.m: then, after that (showing sequence of creation stages with time gaps between them)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows sequence with a significant time gap between events. In this ayah, it connects different stages of human development (from dust, to a drop, to a clinging substance, etc.), emphasizing that each stage takes time before the next begins.

steps.m: from/of (connects نُّطْفَةٍ to what comes after)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it's used to show the stages of human development, connecting نُّطْفَةٍ (drop of fluid) to the next stage.

steps.m: to cling or stick to something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb. In this ayah, this describes the action of clinging that happens in embryonic development.

steps.m: a blood clot (one of the stages mentioned in مِن)
steps.n: The ة changes the clinging action into a noun describing the clinging thing itself - specifically referring to the blood clot stage of embryonic development mentioned in this ayah
The tanwin kasra (تنوين كسرة) shows this word comes after the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah, describing one of the stages of embryonic development

steps.m: then (shows next step in نُطْفَةٍ development)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) with shadda (ّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows the sequence of human development, connecting the stages from dust to نُطْفَةٍ (drop of fluid).

steps.m: from (shows نُّطْفَةٍ is what we were created from)
steps.n: This is one of the most common prepositions in Arabic. Here it shows what humans were created from (a drop of fluid).

steps.m: from (shows نُّطْفَةٍ is what we were created from)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: to chew or bite into small pieces

steps.n: Changes from 'to chew' into 'a chewed piece' - like how food becomes after you chew it

steps.m: a small piece of flesh (like what has been chewed)
steps.n: Added ة to show this is one specific piece

steps.m: a small piece of flesh (affected by مِن before it)
steps.n: The word gets a shadda and kasratain because مِن came before it - this shows it's being affected by مِن
Past tense verb meaning 'he created/shaped'. The root letters got vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it a complete word.
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly or completely
Adding مُ at start and changing vowels shows this describes something that was formed, rather than doing the forming
Adding ة makes this describe a feminine thing (in this case, referring to مُضْغَة - embryo)
The ٍ at the end shows this word is affected by مِن (from/of) before it, and the ّ on م shows it blends with the previous letter
Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'other than' or 'without'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to مُخَلَّقَةٍ (formed) in the ayah
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to create or shape something precisely

steps.n: Added shadda (double letter) to make the meaning stronger - shows creating something with extra care and precision

steps.m: completely formed/shaped (as a result of the action)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to form) into a word describing something that was formed/shaped by someone else
Added feminine ending because it describes مُّضْغَةٍ (lump) which is feminine

steps.m: completely formed/shaped (feminine, describing مُّضْغَةٍ/lump, in a possessive or descriptive phrase)
steps.n: Added genitive case marker because it follows مِن (from) in the verse

steps.m: to make something clear or evident

steps.n: Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show intensity - making something actively clear rather than just being clear
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Adds لِ prefix to indicate purpose ('in order to')
Adds emphasis through doubling of second root letter ي
This is the basic preposition that shows something is being directed towards someone or something
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كم to show who the action is directed towards

steps.m: to settle or keep in place

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter adds emphasis to show something is firmly settled
Adding نُ changes it to passive voice - something is being settled by someone else
Added 'wa' (and) at start + changed to 'we' form meaning 'we keep/maintain'
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Used with رَيْبٍ (doubt) in this ayah to mean 'in doubt'
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a common spelling for this preposition
Added vowels to form the singular noun for womb, which is the base word before making it plural
Changes singular 'womb' to plural 'wombs' using a common plural pattern
Adds a small line under the last letter to show it belongs to or is connected to something

This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb نَشَآءُ (we will) to mean 'whatever we will'. In this ayah, it's talking about whatever Allah wills regarding how long each person stays in the womb.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters combine and the middle letter ي changes to ا (alif) to make the past tense 'he wanted'
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, changed ending to ُ to show it's happening now, and lengthened the ا to آ for emphasis
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is a spelling variation of the long 'aa' sound at the end

steps.m: to, towards (connecting to أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى - an appointed term)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this word connects to what comes after it in the ayah
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and form the noun for a set time period
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'an' in English) and possessed by something else (the word مُّسَمًّى/named that comes after it)
Added a doubled middle letter (shadda) to show doing lots of naming, and vowels to show it happened in the past
Changed from an action (naming something) to describing what received that action (something that was named) by adding مُ at the start
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م and nunation mark (tanween) at the end to show this is describing a specific appointed time

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time passing in between. In this ayah, it's used to show the different stages of human development, with each stage taking time.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added أ at the beginning to show making something else do the action
Changed to present tense and added 'we' as the doer
Added كُمْ to show who is being brought out
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun for a young child

steps.m: a child/infant (object of نُخْرِجُكُمْ - We bring you out as)

steps.m: then, thereafter (showing sequence with a gap)
steps.n: The damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) strengthen the word to show a longer time gap between events. In this ayah, it's used to show the different stages of human development, with each stage taking time.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with ت prefix and adjusted vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose ('in order to')
Root letters combined into a past tense verb meaning 'he' did the action
Forms comparative/superlative adjective from the root meaning 'strong'
Adds second person plural masculine possession pronoun
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'from'
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: and from you all (connected to مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to the previous phrase مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ (whoever dies)
Basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to people
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after و, but the meaning stays the same. This word is about who passes away.
Added shadda to show intensive meaning
Adding تَ makes this reflexive - the action happens to or affects the subject itself (like 'gather oneself' or 'be gathered')
Adding يُ shows this is happening in the present. The ُ (damma) on يُ shows it's passive.
Added alif maqsura (ى with two dots) at the end because this word ends with a weak letter
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added كُم to show this applies to multiple people being addressed

Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in ن (مِنكُم). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to send back or return something

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb

This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it's used with أَجَلٍ (appointed term) to mean 'until/to a specified time'.
Added an alif that looks like a small line (ٰ) above the ى. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

Added أ at the beginning to show this is the worst or most extreme form. Like how 'big' becomes 'biggest' in English.
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the word الْعُمُرِ (life) that comes right after it, meaning 'worst part of life'
Adding damma vowels changes the root into a noun meaning 'age'
Makes the noun definite by adding the definite article prefix

steps.m: of the lifespan (possessed by أَرْذَلِ - most decrepit state)
steps.n: Shows this word belongs to or is connected to أَرْذَلِ (most decrepit state), forming the phrase 'most decrepit state of life' meaning extreme old age
This particle shows purpose or reason - it tells us why something happens

Adding sukun (ْ) on ي helps connect it to the next part

Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and kept final fatha to show subjunctive mood after لِكَيْلَا (so that not)

steps.m: to be far away from something

Added tanwin (two kasras) at the end to show this is talking about any kind of knowledge. This word comes after بَعْدِ (after) in the ayah.

steps.m: a thing (acted upon by يَعْلَمَ - knows)
steps.n: Combined the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْلَمَ (knows) in this verse.

Added ة at the end to make it feminine since it describes الأرض (earth) which is feminine

Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else. It connects what happened before (the earth being lifeless) with what happens next (when We send down water).

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at beginning and sukun on ن makes it mean 'to cause something to be sent down'
Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action. This word connects to الْمَآءَ (the water) which comes right after it in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
Added ي and sukun (ْ) to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: upon it (referring to الْأَرْضَ - the earth)

Added fatha at the end to show this word receives the action of أَنزَلْنَا (we sent down)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: she/it grew/increased (referring to الْأَرْضَ - the earth)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun to show feminine past tense, since the subject (earth) is feminine. The final ا is dropped when ت is added.

Changes from basic root letters to a past tense verb meaning 'it sprouted'. The vowels show it's a simple past action.
Changes from 'it grew' to 'made it grow'. The أ at start shows someone/something caused the action.

Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous statement

Added a small ن mark (called a 'small noon') after the word because it comes before ب. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything of something. In this ayah, it's describing all types or pairs of beautiful plants.
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to مِن (from) that comes before it in the ayah

a pair or type (owned by كُلِّ)
steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning one unit or type. This word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in this ayah.

steps.m: a pair or type (owned by كُلِّ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any general pair/type, not a specific one

steps.m: beautiful and delightful (describing the plants in زَوْجٍۭ)
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to make an adjective describing how beautiful and delightful something is. In this ayah, it's describing the plants that grow.

The alif is written with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show elongation
Adding ل with kasra changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end reinforces that we're pointing to something far away
This is a basic particle that introduces a new statement and emphasizes its truth

steps.m: because (connects to هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ - He is the Truth)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'because' - it connects this statement to explain the reason for what was mentioned before
Changed the root into the basic noun form for 'a deity'

Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: the deity, the god (in accusative case)
steps.n: Adds fatha mark to show this word is the object of something

The damma shows this word is the predicate (what is being said about) the pronoun هُوَ (He) in هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ (He is the truth)
This is a particle that emphasizes certainty

Added pronoun 'he/it' at the end to refer to Allah, who is described as قَدِيرٌ (capable) in this ayah

Changed spelling due to weak letter rules, but meaning stays the same

steps.n: This is a basic word that tells us something certain or definite is about to be mentioned

steps.m: and indeed/and that (connects to previous statement)

steps.n: This root relates to human companionship and society
Adding ا changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'people'

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل with a shadda to show this is the one true deity. The fatha at the end shows Allah is being acted upon in this sentence.
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This special verb only exists in this form. It negates what comes after it. Here it's saying Allah is not an oppressor to His servants.

steps.m: the people (who are being described in this ayah)

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and damma on last two letters. This verb directly acts on اللَّهَ (Allah) in this ayah.
Changed to the unique name for The One True God by adding shadda and changing the first letter

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَعْبُدُ - worships)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being worshipped. Also changed the initial alif to a wasla since it comes after another word.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or about something else. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.

steps.m: to be at the edge or limit of something

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'a specific edge' instead of the action of being at an edge
Added two dots under (ٍ) to show it means 'on an edge' after the word على
Basic conditional word that introduces a condition about what happens to someone
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before
Added basic past tense vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter).
Adding 'أ' at the start changes 'hit' to mean 'caused to hit/strike' or 'befell'. Like turning 'hit' into 'made something hit'
The هُۥ (hu) was added at the end to mean 'him' - showing who was struck or affected
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second to form a noun meaning 'good' or 'better'
Added tanween damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the phrase 'if good befalls him'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding اِ at start makes it a command to someone

steps.m: he became completely at peace (with خَيْرٌ - good fortune)
steps.n: Added shadda at end to show completeness of the action. The hamza in اِ is written as ٱ. This word shows how the person became completely content when good fortune (خَيْرٌ) came to them.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added هِ to show what is being referred to (it)

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to خَيْرٌ - good/goodness)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide. This word refers back to خَيْرٌ (good/goodness) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces what might happen to someone.

steps.m: and if (connects to فَإِنْ in previous ayah)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous one in the ayah. It shows another possible situation being discussed.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Added أَ at beginning to show something causing an effect. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Added تْ to show the subject is feminine (referring to فِتْنَةٌ - trial/test)
Added هُ (him) as the person being affected by the trial
Added the vowel marks to change it from an action to a noun meaning the act itself
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of testing

steps.m: a trial/test (subject of أَصَابَتْهُ - hit/afflicted him)
steps.n: Added the double-damma (tanwin) to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. It's what 'hit/afflicted' him in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: he turned himself away (from the truth)
steps.n: The ٱن at the beginning shows that he did this action to himself. In this ayah, it describes someone who turns away from faith when faced with difficulties.
This is a basic preposition that shows position or manner. Here it describes how the worship (يَعْبُدُ) is done - in a wavering/unstable way.
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the end of the word. It doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the physical face
Added kasra to show this word is connected to something after it
Added هِۦ at the end to show it belongs to him. This word comes after عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb about experiencing a state
Added fatha at the end to complete the past tense verb form
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the quality of being close
Changed و to ي and added ا to make feminine form meaning 'closest/nearest', which came to mean 'worldly' because this life is closer than the next life
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the worldly life' instead of just 'worldly life'
Changes root 'delay/come after' into 'one who comes after/last' by adding ا between first two letters
Adds ة to make the word feminine, often used to refer to 'the hereafter' in Islamic context
Added definite article ال to specify 'the afterlife'
Added conjunction وَ to connect with previous word الدُّنْيَا (worldly life) and added َ at the end to show this is what was lost
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are close by.
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding ل with kasra (ِ ) changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to خَسِرَ - losing/loss)
steps.n: The ك at the end makes it more specific in pointing to something. In this ayah, it's pointing to the loss mentioned right before it (خَسِرَ).
This is a pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers back to ذٰلِكَ (that), which points to the loss mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to lose or suffer loss (especially in trade/business/life)

steps.n: Adding ان to the root with the vowels ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon) turns 'to lose' into 'total loss' - like turning 'break' into 'breakage'

steps.m: the complete loss (subject of هُوَ/it is)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this word is what is being described as هُوَ (it is) in the ayah. The ال makes it specific.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix makes it a participle - describing something that makes things clear

steps.m: the clear, the obvious (describing ٱلْخُسْرَانُ/the loss)
steps.n: The ٱلْ prefix makes it specific and the ُ ending shows this word is describing 'the loss' mentioned in the ayah
Added fathas to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
The ي at the start shows it's happening now and 'he' is doing it
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or away from something. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَدْعُوا (he calls/invokes) with دُونِ (besides/other than).
Added a kasra (ِ) mark under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Adding damma (ُ) changes it from a root to a usable word
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word after it (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'besides Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes it, and the kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon by something before it in the sentence.
A primitive particle used to ask 'what' or refer to 'that which'. Not derived from a root.

steps.m: that which (object of يَدْعُوا۟ - he calls upon)
steps.n: Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, مَا refers to whatever false deities or idols are being called upon besides Allah.
Basic negative particle that makes the verb يَضُرُّهُ (harms him) into 'does not harm him'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to ُ, and combined repeated ر with shadda
Added هُ at the end to show who is being harmed. This word comes after لا (not) which negates it.
Basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: and what (that is worshipped besides اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it refers to false deities that are worshipped besides Allah.
This is a basic word that makes things negative. Here it's saying something does NOT benefit him.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end

steps.m: he benefits him (object of يَدْعُوا - what he calls upon)
steps.n: Added هُۥ to show who receives the benefit. This refers back to what is being called upon (مَا) in the phrase يَدْعُوا (he calls upon)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. It doesn't come from a root.
The alif is written above the ذ (called an overextended alif) to show reaching further away
Adding لِ makes it point to something far away instead of nearby
Adding كَ completes the word, making it the standard way to say 'that' when pointing to something far away

This is a personal pronoun that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to masculine things or people. In this ayah, it refers to what was mentioned before (calling upon other than Allah) and connects to what comes after (being far astray).

steps.m: to wander off the right path
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding alif makes the straying more permanent or continuous
Doubling the first letter shows how deep or severe the straying is
Adding definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: the deep, far straying (subject of هُوَ - it is)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the predicate (what something is) after هُوَ (it is) in the ayah
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make an adjective describing something that is distant

steps.m: the far one (describing something that is very distant from the truth)
steps.n: Makes the word specific and definite. In this ayah, it's describing how far astray someone has gone from the truth.
Added fathas to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
The prefix ي and the change in vowels shows this changes from past tense (he called) to present tense (he calls)
Starting with the basic particle combination of ل and من
Adding fatha on ل changes it from a simple 'to' into an emphasis particle meaning 'surely/indeed'
Adding fatha on م completes the word. This word is the subject of يَدْعُوا۟ (he calls/invokes) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel to make it passive - something happened TO the person
Changed from verb 'was harmed' to noun 'harm' and added هُۥٓ (his) at the end to show whose harm. This harm is being described as 'closer' in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changed from 'was near' to 'nearer/closer' to compare two things - like saying something is closer than something else
This is a preposition used here to compare two things: his harm (ضَرُّهُۥٓ) and his benefit (نَّفْعِهِۦ). It shows that one thing is closer/nearer than the other.
Changed verb into noun by using the basic noun pattern
Adds possessive pronoun 'his' to show who the benefit belongs to. The word comes after مِن (than) in the ayah.
Changes from basic root to past tense verb expressing disapproval
Adds لَ to emphasize how bad something is

steps.m: to be close to and protect/support

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ى at the end to make it mean 'the one who protects' instead of just 'to protect'. Like changing 'help' to 'helper'.

steps.m: the protector (who is being called upon in vain)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific protector. This word refers to false deities or protectors that some people call upon, even though these can't help them.
Added vowel marks to create the basic form that expresses strong disapproval
Added لَ to emphasize how bad something is

steps.m: and surely how bad! (describing المولى - the protector)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this criticism with the previous statement about calling on those who harm more than benefit
Adding ي between letters with these vowels creates a word for someone who does this action a lot or has this quality strongly
Adding definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: the close companion (subject of يَدْعُوا۟/he calls upon)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action in يَدْعُوا۟ (calls upon/invokes)
This is a special word that adds emphasis to the statement that follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah (اللَّهَ) will definitely do something.
Changed the root into the word for 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is affected by something before it in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif at the beginning makes it causative - now it means making something enter rather than entering yourself

steps.m: he causes to enter (doer is الله)
steps.n: Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. In this ayah, Allah is the one doing the action of causing to enter.
Added ال to make it definite and point to something specific. The ل gets a shadda because it combines with the first letter of the word.

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned after)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) that comes after it.
Changed the root أمن into past tense ءَامَنَ by adding ءَا at the start to mean 'he believed/had faith' rather than just feeling safe
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is describing who Allah will admit to Paradise in this verse.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do deeds

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he did'
Adding و at beginning connects this word with ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) in the previous phrase, showing these are two connected actions: 'believed and did'

steps.m: and they did/worked (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ - those who believed)
steps.n: Adding plural masculine suffix وا shows this refers to the same group mentioned in ءَامَنُوا۟ (those who believed)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to active participle form to mean 'one who is righteous/good'. This word is the object (what is being done) of the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed).
Makes the word definite
Changes from singular 'righteous' to plural 'righteous deeds' by adding -aat ending
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)

steps.m: to cover or hide something completely

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) and doubled the middle letter to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it covered'

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered by trees)
steps.n: Added ة to make it a noun meaning 'a place that is covered' which came to mean 'garden' since gardens are covered by trees
Changed ة to ات to show there are many gardens

steps.m: gardens (being acted upon by يُدْخِلُ - He admits/enters)
steps.n: Added ٍ (tanween kasra) to show these gardens are the object - the place believers are being entered into
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ prefix and changing middle vowel to kasra

This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَجْرِى (flows) with تَحْتِهَا (beneath it), showing that the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
This root represents the concept of being underneath or below something
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable as a noun meaning 'under'
Added هَا to show we're talking about the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) being above. The ِ (kasra) before هَا shows this word is connected to another word with مِن (from) in the ayah.
Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun for a river - something that flows
Changed the word to show we're talking about multiple rivers by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle vowels
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about specific rivers, not just any rivers
Added ُ at the end to show these rivers are doing the action of flowing (تَجْرِى) in this verse

This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It has a shadda (ّ) on the ن to show extra strength. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah definitely does what He wants.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form

steps.m: he does/is doing (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and ُ at end. This word describes what Allah does, as mentioned in إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ (Indeed, Allah)
Basic form of the word that can mean 'what' when asking questions
Added fatha to show this is being used to connect to the next word (يَفْعَلُ) to mean 'whatever He wills'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas

Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowels to match present form pattern, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word is connected to يَفْعَلُ (he does) in this ayah.

This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who thinks/assumes something.
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make the past tense verb 'he was'. This word tells us who did the thinking (يَظُنُّ) in this ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to think or believe something without being completely sure

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form

steps.m: he thinks/believes (about أَن لَّن يَنصُرَهُ ٱللَّهُ - that Allah will not help him)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels to damma, and adding shadda
This particle introduces what someone thinks or believes. Here it connects to يَظُنُّ (thinks/assumes) to show what is being thought.
This is a basic particle that negates future actions. It has no root because it's a helper word.
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger. In this ayah, it's saying Allah will never help such a person.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to damma
Added fatha at end to make it subjunctive mood
Added هُ at the end to show who is being helped. This whole phrase is what someone thinks (يَظُنُّ) won't happen.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: the deity, the one who is worshipped
steps.n: Added 'al' to make it specific/definite, like changing 'a god' to 'the god'
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows location 'in' the worldly life (الدُّنْيَا)
The ى at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. It's like a bridge between words showing where something is located.
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - added vowels and ي to make the word 'worldly' or 'this lower life'

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with وَٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the hereafter)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. The ل combines with د to make دّ. In this ayah, it's specifically contrasted with 'the hereafter' to mean this worldly life.
Changed root letters to make 'last/final' by adding a long aa sound at start and i sound in middle
Added feminine ending ة to create noun referring to the afterlife/hereafter

steps.m: the last life, the life after
steps.n: Adding ال makes the noun definite
Adding و connects this word to previous phrase
Added kasra at end because it follows في (in) and pairs with الدنيا (this world)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added لْ to turn it into a polite command or suggestion
Added فَ to show this action should happen after something else mentioned before

steps.m: to connect or link things together

steps.n: Added vowels to make a noun meaning the physical thing (rope) or abstract thing (means) that connects

steps.m: by/with a rope/means (object of فَلْيَمْدُدْ - let him extend)
steps.n: Added بِ to show this is the thing being used to do the action. This connects to the verb فَلْيَمْدُدْ (let him extend) in the ayah.

steps.m: by/with a rope/means (object of فَلْيَمْدُدْ - let him extend)
steps.n: Added tanween (two dots under) to show this is any general rope/means, not a specific one
This is a preposition that shows direction. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلسَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven) to show where something is going toward.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added hamza to create a noun meaning the place or thing associated with rising up
Added ال to make it definite, with shadda on س because it comes after ال
Added kasra because it follows the preposition إِلَى (to/towards). The alif is written long (آ) in this style.

steps.m: then (connecting لْيَقْطَعْ 'let him cut' to what came before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one action follows another. The shadda (ّ) makes it stronger, like saying 'and then' instead of just 'then'. Here it connects 'let him cut' to the previous instructions.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past tense (he cut) to present tense command form (let him cut) by adding يَ at start and sukun marks
Added لْ to make it a command/suggestion. This appears in a sequence of commands in the ayah.
ي at start shows it's happening now/future
The ُ vowel helps show this is present tense
The ْ at the end shows this is jussive mood
Added لْ at start to make it a command meaning 'let him' do something

steps.m: then let him look (connected to لْيَقْطَعْ - let him cut)
steps.n: Added فَ (then/so) to connect this command with the next action (لْيَقْطَعْ - let him cut) in the verse
This is a question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question. Here it's asking about whether something will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing something to happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added نَّ at the end to show emphasis and certainty
Changed the action (planning secretly) into a thing (a secret plan). This word is what's doing the action in يُذْهِبَنَّ (will remove).
Adds -u at the end to show this secret plan is doing the action
Adds 'his' to show who the plan belongs to. This plan is what's doing the removing in يُذْهِبَنَّ (will remove).
This is a relative pronoun connecting to the verb يَغِيظُ (enrages). It introduces what comes after it as the thing being discussed.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. This word describes the emotional state of the subject of يُذْهِبَنَّ (will remove) in this ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'that'
The word 'that' gets a special لِ added to make it stronger, like pointing at something with extra force
The كَ at the end is a suffix that points to something far away, like saying 'that over there'
Adding كَ (ka) at the start changes 'that' to 'like that' - similar to saying 'exactly like that'
The و connects this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes someone cause the action to happen - like changing 'go down' to 'make go down'
Added نا to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: we sent it down (referring to the clear signs/آيات mentioned after)
steps.n: The هُ at the end means 'it', referring to the آيات (signs) mentioned right after this word in the ayah
Forms the singular noun by combining the root letters and adding ة to show one instance
Forms regular feminine plural by adding ات suffix
The dots under the word (ٍ) show it is receiving the action, like when you say 'I saw the signs'
Added shadda (double mark) on the middle letter to make the meaning stronger
Added ة at the end to make it refer to one specific clear sign or proof
Changed the ending to ٰت to make it refer to multiple clear signs

steps.m: clear signs/proofs (as the object of أَنزَلْنٰهُ)
steps.n: Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'We sent down' (أَنزَلْنٰهُ)
This is a particle that emphasizes the statement that follows it (that Allah guides)
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this clause with what precedes it
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the doubled ل shows emphasis, and the َ at the end shows it's being acted upon in this sentence.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense. The ي at the end becomes ى because it's at the end of the word.

Added ي at start to show present tense. Changed middle letter's vowel to sukun and last letter's vowel to kasra to match present tense pattern. The word shows that Allah is the one doing the guiding in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it's the subject (doer) of the verb يُرِيدُ (wants/desires).
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowels, and added ُ at end to show he is doing the action. The middle ا changes to ي in present tense.
This is a special word that adds emphasis and makes the word after it end in َ (fatha). Here it emphasizes that Allah will judge between them.
Added ال with a shadda to make it specific. This word points to one person or thing that does something.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. This word is connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in this ayah, describing who believed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The addition of alif after first root letter changes meaning to 'believe'
Changed from 'he believed' to 'they believed' by adding وا at the end, showing multiple people believing instead of one person
This is the basic relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the person who...' or 'the thing which...'
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding و at the beginning connects this to the previous phrase. The other marks (َ ٱ ّ ِ) help with pronunciation.
Added vowels and the middle weak letter و changed to ا, which is common in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the middle letter to make صابِئ (one who turns away) then added ين to make it plural (many who turn away)
Adding definite article to specify this particular group
Adding coordinating conjunction to connect with previous elements
Adds extra ص and ا to make it mean 'someone who helps a lot' (like a job title)
Changed from a word meaning 'helper' to the special plural word for Christians by adding ى at the end (with an alif before it)
Added definite article ال which causes the ن to get a shadda
Adding conjunction to connect to previous phrase
Changes from action to group of people who do the action
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the Magians' instead of just 'Magians'
Added و with fatha to connect this word with the previous groups mentioned in the verse

steps.m: and the Magians (object in this verse)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is an object in the list of groups being mentioned. Also changed hamza to wasl form as is standard.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'who' or 'which' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding و at the beginning connects this with the previous phrase, like saying 'and'. The other changes (ٱ and َ) help with pronunciation.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added 'أ' at the start changes meaning from 'shared' to 'made something share/join' - like turning a simple action into making something do that action
Changes from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end
Added kasra (ِ) and shadda (ّ) to make it an emphasis word that strengthens what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah (ٱللَّهَ) will judge between them.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (the direct object of يَفْصِلُ - He judges)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence. Also added the shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed the initial alif to a wasla since it comes in the middle of speech.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb form
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This verb shows what Allah does to distinguish between the different groups mentioned.
A small 'u' sound (damma) was added at the end to show this is happening in the present

steps.m: to separate or distinguish between things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this into the common word meaning 'between' that connects things

steps.m: between them (referring to the groups mentioned: those who believed, Jews, Sabians, Christians, Magians, and polytheists)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this applies to all the groups Allah mentioned before
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: on the day (when Allah will judge between them)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَفْصِلُ (He will judge). It tells us when the judging will happen.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'stood' into noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added ة to make it refer to a specific instance - the resurrection
Added the definite article to specify THE resurrection
Changed to 'of resurrection' by adding kasra at the end since it follows 'day of' (يَوْمَ) in the ayah
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it (Allah) stronger and more certain.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the proper name for The One True God

steps.m: Allah (the direct object of يَفْصِلُ - He judges)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence. The verb يَفْصِلُ (He judges) is doing the action to Allah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is higher than or on top of something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ meaning 'thing')
This word means everything without exception
Added damma and shadda to create a word meaning 'every' or 'all' from the idea of completeness
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'of every thing'
This root represents the basic concept of something existing or being a thing
The root letters were connected to make a noun meaning 'thing', with a fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. The hamza at the end shows it's a single thing.

steps.m: of a thing (owned by كُلِّ - every)
steps.n: Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every). Together they form كُلِّ شَىْءٍ meaning 'of every thing'. Also changed the ي to ى because it's followed by hamza.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes someone who does the action. This word is connected to يَفْصِلُ (judges/decides between) in this ayah.
The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is a standalone word meaning 'a witness', rather than connecting to another word in the sentence
This particle turns a statement into a question, similar to adding '?' in English. Here it's asking about the verb تَرَ (you see)
Added لَ which makes the question negative, like asking 'did not?' instead of just 'did?'
Added مْ which completes the past tense negative meaning. The whole phrase أَلَمْ تَرَ means 'Did you not see?'

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed ending vowel to show present tense
The weak letter أ changes to ى in present tense
The ى at the end is removed because أَلَمْ (meaning 'did not') comes before it - this is a common pattern in Arabic

steps.m: that (connecting أَلَمْ تَرَ to what follows)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links 'Do you not see' to what follows. It helps introduce what exactly you should see.
Changed first letter to ل and added shadda to make it specific to The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَسْجُدُ - prostrates to)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being prostrated to, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for. This word connects to يَسْجُدُ (prostrates) to show who is being prostrated to.
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to those who prostrate to Allah.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows who is in the heavens (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ).
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The final و changes to ء because it comes at the end of a noun after a long alif
The word for 'sky' becomes plural by adding ات at the end. The ء (hamza) at the end changes back to و (waw) before adding ات
Adding ال makes the word 'the heavens' instead of just 'heavens'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word follows في (in) in the ayah
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (who/whoever)
This is a basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it's specifically talking about those who are in the heavens.
Connected the letters together and added basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific, and 'i' at the end because it follows the word فِي (in/on)

steps.n: The root letters combine to form the basic noun for sun

steps.m: to shine brightly like the moon

Added ال to make it specific
Added و at the beginning to connect it to the previous words in the verse
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is one of the subjects doing the prostrating (يَسْجُدُ) mentioned earlier in the verse

Base meaning of covering or concealing
Adding vowel marks changes it from a root to a proper word
Changes from 'to cover' into 'a covered/enclosed place' - like a garden with trees covering it
Makes the word plural to show many gardens
The -in ending shows these gardens are the object (something is happening to them)
Base meaning of flowing or running
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to feminine form since it refers to rivers flowing under gardens (جَنَّاتٍ)

steps.m: from/of (showing what أَسَاوِرَ 'bracelets' are made of)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing what something is made from. In this ayah, it shows the bracelets (أَسَاوِرَ) are made of gold.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it pronounceable. The َ (fatha) and ْ (sukun) show how to say the word.
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this word connects to something that comes after it
Added هَا at the end to show we're talking about the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form for a single river
This is the broken plural form showing multiple rivers
The ال prefix makes it definite ('the rivers'), and the ُ shows it's the subject of the verb تَجْرِى (flows) in this ayah
Base meaning of adorning
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling middle letter (shadda)
Added يُ at the beginning to make it passive - showing something is being decorated by someone else
Added و to show multiple people (they) are being decorated
Added نَ to complete the plural form for 'they'
Basic preposition showing location or containment. This word has no root since it's a particle.
Adding the kasra vowel mark under ف to show how it's pronounced
Added ها at the end to show this refers to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse. The ها is feminine because جَنَّاتٍ is feminine.

steps.m: of/from (showing the bracelets are made of ذَهَبٍ gold)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing what something is made of. Here it connects bracelets (أَسَاوِرَ) with gold (ذَهَبٍ).
This is the basic concept of surrounding something
Changes to a noun meaning the physical object that surrounds (like a bracelet)
Changes from one type of plural (سِوَر) to another type of plural (أَسَاوِر) - both mean multiple bracelets

steps.m: bracelets (that they will be adorned with in Paradise)
steps.n: The extra mark at the end shows these bracelets are being received or given to someone

steps.m: of/from (describing what أَسَاوِرَ 'bracelets' are made of)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing what something is made of. In this ayah, it's telling us the bracelets are made of gold.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected letters with fatha to form the noun for the metal gold
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes what the bracelets are made of after the word 'min' (from/of)
Added the basic vowel marks to connect the letters and make the word pronounceable
Adds 'and' to connect this with the gold bracelets mentioned before (أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ)
The added ending ًا shows this is a direct object (something being given), matching with the bracelets mentioned before

steps.m: to put on, to cover with
steps.n: This is the basic concept of wearing something
Root meaning 'to wear' becomes a noun meaning the thing you wear (clothing)
Adds the pronoun 'their' for multiple people. This clothing is what's being described as being حَرِيرٌ (silk) in the ayah.
Adds 'and' at the beginning
This is a basic preposition showing location inside the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier

steps.m: in them (referring to جَنَّاتٍ - the gardens)
steps.n: Adds ها to show we're talking about the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier. Since جَنَّاتٍ (gardens) is feminine plural, we use the feminine 'it/them' form ها

steps.m: to be pure, smooth, or free
steps.n: This root relates to freedom and purity
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is something with the quality of being pure and smooth

steps.m: a silk (garment mentioned in وَلِبَاسُهُمْ - their clothing)
steps.n: Adding ٌ shows this is any silk garment, not a specific one. This word describes what type of clothing (لِبَاس) the people of Paradise will wear.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter.
Changed vowels to show that the action happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا at the end to show many people were guided. The weak letter ي drops out when we add وا.

steps.m: and they were guided (to صِرٰطِ ٱلْحَمِيدِ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the start to mean 'and'. The word connects to 'the path of the Praiseworthy' later in the verse.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'they were guided' (هُدُوٓا۟) with 'the path' (صِرٰطِ)
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is just how the word is written and doesn't change its meaning.
The vowels show this describes a temporary quality
Added the definite article ال which changes to ٱل and causes the first letter to double

steps.m: of the pure, of the good (describing القَوْلِ - the speech)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word describes 'the speech' (القَوْلِ) in this ayah
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْقَوْلِ (the speech/word). This combination means 'from the speech'
Changed from action to noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: to guide (to show someone the right way)
he was guided (someone showed him the right way)
steps.n: Changed the word to show that someone received guidance instead of giving it. The ُ (u) on هُ and ِ (i) on دِ show this change.

steps.m: they were guided (someone showed them the right way)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people were guided, not just one person. Also changed the middle vowel to ُ (u) because of this ending.
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, meaning 'and'. The ٓ (long alif) and ۟ (small circle) are just pronunciation helpers.
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects هُدُوا (they were guided) to صِرٰطِ (path), showing where they were guided to.
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end which is a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning
This common pattern makes nouns that mean a path or way of doing something
Changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) and added kasra at the end to show this path belongs to الحميد (The Praiseworthy). This is a common Quranic spelling variation.

steps.m: to praise or commend something good
one who is worthy of praise
steps.n: Added ي in the middle to show this describes someone who deserves praise, like changing 'praise' to 'praiseworthy'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end to show it's connected to صِرٰطِ (path) before it, meaning 'path of the Praiseworthy One'
This is the basic form of the emphasis word that makes statements stronger

Adding the kasra (ِ) under the first letter and shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger. It's like adding an exclamation mark in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about those who disbelieve.
This is the basic form used to point to something or someone. It's like saying 'this' or 'that' in English.
Added ال with a shadda to make it specific and point to particular people or things being discussed.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) by describing who did the action.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth
he rejected/denied (subject of يَصُدُّونَ - they prevent)
