steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb. This word works with يَصُدُّونَ (they prevent) in this ayah.

steps.m: they rejected/denied (subject of يَصُدُّونَ - they prevent)
steps.n: Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (masculine plural)'. This matches with the plural verb يَصُدُّونَ that follows.
The doubled middle letter (ّ) shows this blocking is done forcefully or repeatedly
Adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels shows this blocking is happening in the present time
Adding ونَ shows multiple people (those who disbelieve) are doing the blocking

steps.m: and they block/prevent (with force) (connected to كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous word كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved), showing these are the same people doing both actions
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance. In this ayah, it shows people being turned away from سَبِيلِ (the path of Allah).
Adding ي and vowels creates the pattern for 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this path belongs to Allah (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ means 'path of Allah')
Added emphasis to the middle letter to show the special nature of this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word is possessed by سَبِيلِ (path) in the phrase 'path of Allah'

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ِ after ج to create a noun meaning 'place where prostration happens'
Added ال to make it specific. The alif becomes ٱ because it connects to the previous word.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added ِ at the end to show this mosque is possessed by or connected to something (in this case الحرام - the Sacred)
Added ا after second root letter to create a noun meaning something that is completely sacred or forbidden
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added kasra at the end because this word describes المسجد (the mosque) which has kasra, so they must match
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it refers back to مَسْجِدِ (mosque) and connects it to what comes after - that we made it equal for all people.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نا to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: we made it (referring to المسجد الحرام - the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what was made/set. The هُ refers back to the Sacred Mosque mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to be friendly or familiar with others
people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'people' by adding ا after the first letter. The word نَاس comes from the idea of humans being social creatures who are familiar with each other.
The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to the word before it, like saying 'of the people'
Added ال which becomes ٱلْ to make it specific people. The ن gets doubled (شدّة) because it combines with the ل
Added لِ at the start to mean 'for'. When لِ comes before ال, the ل of ال disappears but leaves its effect (the shadda)
Added vowels (َ) to س and و, and changed ي to ا at the end to make the word mean 'equal'
Added hamza after alif to make it a noun describing a quality
Added tanween to show this word describes how something is done

steps.m: to stay or devote oneself to something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action. This describes someone who stays in or devotes themselves to the Sacred Mosque.
Added the definite article to specify we're talking about a particular person who stays
The word gets a 'u' ending (ُ) to show it's the subject, and the long 'a' is written with a straight line (ٰ) instead of an alif
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in it (referring to المَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ - the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه which refers back to the Sacred Mosque mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to live in the desert/to be nomadic
one who lives in the desert (like العاكف)
steps.n: The root ب-د-و (desert living) becomes بَادٍ (desert dweller) - like changing 'nomad' to 'nomadic person'. This person is mentioned alongside العاكف (city dweller) to show both types are equal in the Sacred Mosque.

steps.m: the desert dweller (equal with العاكف in rights at the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added the definite article ٱلْ to make the word specific
Added conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous phrase
This is a relative pronoun that refers to any person. It's like saying 'the one who' or 'anyone who' in English.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word is the subject (doer) of يُرِدْ (intends) in this ayah.
Added يُ at start to mean 'he', and changed vowels to show ongoing action of wanting/intending something

steps.m: he wants/intends (in the context of whoever wants/intends)
steps.n: The و drops out and is replaced with a sukun (ْ) because this is a conditional sentence starting with 'whoever' (مَن)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to الْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ - the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the Sacred Mosque (الْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ) mentioned earlier.
The root refers to turning away from what is right or proper
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the act of turning away by adding إِ at start and ا after ح
Added بِ to show this is done 'with' or 'by' something
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon by the verb يُرِدْ (intends/desires)

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm someone's rights
steps.n: This is the basic past tense form
Changed the word from an action (to be unfair) into a thing (unfairness)
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how something is done. This word explains what kind of wrongdoing (بِإِلْحَادٍۭ) is being talked about in the ayah.

steps.m: with unfairness (describing the wrongdoing in بِإِلْحَادٍۭ)
steps.n: Added the mark ٍ at the end to show this word is connected to and describes بِإِلْحَادٍۭ (wrongdoing) in the ayah
The basic verb form
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added hamza at beginning to show causative meaning - making someone else do the action
Changed to present tense first person plural
Added emphasis (doubled ن) and shortened the word to show it's conditional (if/when something happens)

steps.m: we will make him taste/experience (referring to punishment in this ayah)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix to show who receives the action

steps.m: from (connects عَذَابٍ 'punishment' to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word 'punishment' to the previous part.
The root relates to punishment or torment
Changed from action to the result of the action (like changing 'punish' to 'punishment' in English)
The ٍ shows this is 'a punishment' (not specific) and comes after the word 'from' (مِنْ) in the ayah
The root relates to experiencing pain
Adding ي between second and third root letters makes this describe something with a very strong quality of causing pain
The tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end shows this is describing an undefined punishment (عَذَابٍ). Like saying "a painful punishment" in English.
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. Here it introduces when Allah assigned the location to Ibrahim.

steps.m: and when (connecting to بَوَّأْنَا - We assigned)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the Quran, adding the meaning of 'and'
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to really settle someone' instead of just 'to settle'
The suffix نا shows 'we' did this action. This word connects to إِبْرٰهِيمَ (Ibrahim) who receives the place.
This is a proper name that came from Hebrew into Arabic. It refers to Prophet Abraham, who built the Ka'bah.
Added ل with kasra (لِ) at the beginning to show this is being given or assigned to Abraham

steps.m: to/for Abraham (receiver of بَوَّأْنَا - We assigned)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Abraham is receiving the action of بَوَّأْنَا (We assigned/established)

steps.m: to exist or be in a place
steps.n: The و is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed
Added مَ at the beginning to show a place, like how adding مَ to 'write' gives us 'desk' (place of writing). This is a common pattern for making place words in Arabic.
Changed و to ا because it's a weak letter, and added ا after ك to make it a place noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by بَوَّأْنَا (We assigned/prepared)
The root becomes a noun meaning the place where you spend the night
Root letters put into common pattern for places/buildings - like turning 'live' into 'living place'
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this belongs to or follows after مَكَانَ (place/location), making مَكَانَ ٱلْبَيْتِ mean 'the place of the house'
This is a basic connecting word that introduces what someone says, thinks, or what happens next

steps.m: that (introduces what comes after وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا)
steps.n: Added the fatha mark (َ) above أ to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it connects to the earlier phrase 'And when We assigned' to introduce what was assigned.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'

Adding the shadda (ّ) on the ل makes the negation stronger. This emphasizes that something absolutely must not happen. Here it connects to تُشْرِكْ (associate partners) to say 'absolutely do not associate partners'.
Noun form meaning the act of associating partners with God
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he did'
Removed first vowel, changed middle vowel to 'i', and added exclamation mark - makes it a command to one person
Added 'tu' at start to make it mean 'you'
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected

steps.m: with me (referring to بَيْتِىَ - my house)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This connects to the word بَيْتِىَ (my house) in the ayah, showing who the house belongs to.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is what's being acted upon by the verb تُشْرِكْ (associate/join in worship).
Added ًا (tanween fatha) to show this is the object being acted upon
Root meaning of purity
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show doing something thoroughly

steps.m: and purify! (command to purify بَيْتِىَ - My House)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command with previous commands in the verse
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun for a dwelling place
Added kasra to prepare for the possessive suffix
Added the first person possessive suffix ى with fatha to show ownership
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from verb (action) to noun (person doing action)
Added ين to show we're talking about many people who go around
Added ال to show we're talking about specific people who go around, and doubled the ط with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: for the ones who go around (connected to بَيْتِىَ)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this is for or belongs to these people. This connects to the word بَيْتِىَ (my house) showing who the house is for.
The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a word for someone who does the action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people who stand
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Connects this word to the previous words in the verse
Root meaning of bowing

steps.m: those who bow down frequently in worship
steps.n: Changes to plural form showing people who do lots of bowing

steps.m: the ones who bow frequently in worship
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which makes the word specific and adds a double mark (shadda) to the first letter

steps.m: and the ones who bow frequently in worship
steps.n: Added conjunction و to connect this to previous words about those who worship at the House (الْبَيْت)

steps.m: and the ones who bow frequently in worship (connected to لِلطَّآئِفِينَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this word is connected grammatically to لِلطَّآئِفِينَ (for those who circle) from earlier in the verse

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground
steps.n: Basic past tense form
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to noun form by adding damma on first and second letters and و between them. This changes the action into the name of the action itself.
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added i-sound at the end because it comes after the word 'of' and connects to 'those who bow' in the verse
The shadda makes it a stronger command to announce or proclaim to many people

steps.m: and announce! (as a command to Prophet Ibrahim)
steps.n: Adding و connects this command to previous instructions. This is Allah commanding Prophet Ibrahim to make an announcement about Hajj.

steps.m: in (connects ٱلنَّاسِ to the verb أَذِّن)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسِ) to the command 'announce' (أَذِّن), showing who to announce to.

steps.m: to be social or familiar with others
people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changes from verb (to be social) to noun (people who are social)
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people'
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the preposition فِى (in)
The middle letter is doubled to show importance/emphasis
Adding definite article to specify this is the religious pilgrimage
Added بِ to show this is about proclaiming/announcing the pilgrimage (connects to وَأَذِّن/announce in the ayah)
Added يَ at beginning to show it's happening now or in the future, and ِ to connect the ending
Added و to show multiple people (men) are doing the action

steps.m: they come to you (referring to the people who will come for Hajj)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show the action is directed towards 'you' (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
Changes the singular 'man' into a plural form meaning 'men'

steps.m: men (as the ones who come walking in يَأْتُوكَ)
steps.n: Added ا and ً to make it 'men' in a way that shows they are doing the walking/coming
Starting with the basic word for 'upon'. The small alif (ىٰ) at the end shows this is the complete form of the word.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous verb يَأْتُوكَ (they come to you). It shows that people come both walking and riding.
The doubled ل with damma makes it mean 'every' or 'all' - like saying something includes everything with no exceptions
The kasra at the end shows this word is describing فَجٍّ (path/road) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a describing word by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter

steps.m: a lean one (describing a riding animal in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds tanwin kasra at the end to show this is describing an unspecified riding animal mentioned in the context
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, and added vowel marks to connect the letters
Added نَ at the end to show multiple females are doing the action. This word is part of a description of how people will come to perform Hajj.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'they come' (يَأْتِينَ) with 'every deep valley' (كُلِّ فَجٍّ), showing where the people come from.
The doubled ل shows completeness, while the ُ makes it into a noun
The ِ shows this word is connected to على (upon) and describes ضَامِرٍ (lean mount)

steps.m: to spread apart widely, make spacious

steps.n: Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter ج to show intensity of width
Added ٍ (kasratan) at the end to show this is any wide path, not a specific one. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
Basic root letters got vowels added to make a past tense verb meaning 'it became deep'
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it into an adjective describing something as deep
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes the valley (فَجٍّ) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it 'he witnessed' in past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why they are doing the action
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and small high waw (۟) as a pronunciation guide
Changed from a verb root into a noun by adding vowel marks (fatha and sukun)
Added مَ at the start to show where or how the benefit happens
Added ا after first letter to make it plural, showing multiple benefits
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object (thing being witnessed) in relation to لِيَشْهَدُوا (so that they may witness)
Added fatha to the letter ل to show it's connected to what comes after it
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple people). This shows who the benefits are for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people and subjunctive mood (because it follows لِ meaning 'so that they')
Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this action with the previous verb لِّيَشْهَدُوا۟ (so that they witness)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from verb 'he named' to the noun 'name'. Like changing 'named' to 'name' in English. This is the thing being mentioned in يَذْكُرُوا۟ (they mention).
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to something else (the word اسْمَ meaning 'name' that comes before it). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.

steps.m: in (shows when something happens during أَيَّامٍ - days)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows when something happens. Here it connects to the word أَيَّامٍ (days) to tell us when people should remember Allah's name.

steps.m: in (shows when something happens during أَيَّامٍ - days)
steps.n: The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing rule and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun
Adding أ at start is first step to make plural
Double the middle letter and add alif at end to complete plural
Add fatha marks to complete the plural pattern
The word is now in genitive case, shown by the -in ending
Adds two dots under the word (tanween kasra) to show these are specific days being talked about
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from 'to know' to 'known/understood' by adding مَ at start and making it passive with و
Adds emphasis by doubling the م sound at the beginning
Makes it plural to show multiple known things or matters
Shows this word is connected to 'days' (أَيَّامٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else. It's like saying 'on' in English.
The small mark (ٰ) above the ى shows this is pronounced like a regular alif. In this ayah, it connects what Allah provided (رَزَقَهُم) with what was provided (بَهِيمَةِ/livestock).

steps.m: that which (connects to رَزَقَهُم - He provided them)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links to what comes after it. Here it connects to رَزَقَهُم (He provided them) to mean 'that which He provided them'.

steps.m: that which (connects to رَزَقَهُم - He provided them)
steps.n: Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he provided them (with what was needed)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who received the provision
This is a helper word that connects other words. It shows something comes from or is part of something else.
The م gets doubled (shown by ّ) because it comes after another word ending in ل. This makes it easier to say.
A small ن (called tanween) is added at the end to help connect this word smoothly to the next word.

steps.m: to be unable to speak clearly or communicate

steps.n: Added ي and vowels to turn the root meaning 'unclear speech' into an adjective describing something that is completely dark/black (where nothing is clear)
Added ة to turn the general word for 'unable to speak clearly' into a noun meaning 'animal' (because animals can't speak)
Added kasra at the end to show this animal belongs to or comes from Allah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb about blessing into a plural noun meaning blessed animals like cows and sheep
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Shows this word belongs to or comes after something else in the sentence
Changed to basic command form by removing initial أ and adding damma
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added فَ to connect this command with what came before it in the verse - they should witness benefits and remember Allah's name, so then eat from the animals
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from
Added ها at the end to show this means 'from it' where 'it' refers back to something feminine mentioned before (بَهِيمَةِ ٱلْأَنْعٰمِ - livestock animals)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أ makes it causative - 'make someone eat'
Changes to present tense - 'I feed' instead of 'he fed'
Changes from 'I feed' to a command for multiple people to feed

steps.m: to be in hardship or difficulty
one who is in hardship/poor person
steps.n: Changes from verb (being in hardship) to noun (person in hardship)

steps.m: the one who is in hardship
steps.n: Makes it specific which poor person we're talking about

steps.m: the one who is in hardship (object of أَطْعِمُوا۟)
steps.n: 1. The hamza at the start becomes a wasl mark (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word. 2. The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of feeding.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Forms an adjective describing someone characterized by poverty
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of feeding (object of the verb feed/أَطْعِمُوا۟)
Basic form of this connecting word that shows sequence of events
Added damma (ُ), fatha (َ), and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. This word connects to the next action لْيَقْضُوا۟ (let them complete), showing it happens after previous actions.

steps.m: to complete, to fulfill, to carry out

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the root letters. The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end after a fatha.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لْ to make it a command/request
Added وا to make it plural (they) instead of singular (he)

steps.m: cleaning up, removal of impurities (object of لْيَقْضُوا۟)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form a noun. This word is what is being completed/fulfilled in the command لْيَقْضُوا۟ (let them complete).

steps.m: their cleaning up, their removal of impurities (object of لْيَقْضُوا۟)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this belongs to them (the pilgrims). The ْ (sukun) at the end shows this is connected to what comes next in the ayah.

steps.m: to fulfill or complete a promise/obligation

steps.n: Added يُ at the start to show 'he' is doing the action, and vowel marks to make it a present tense verb meaning 'fulfills'
Added لْ at beginning to make it a command/request
Added و (and) to connect this command with previous phrase
Added وا to make it plural - now means multiple people should fulfill
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Changes from one promise to many promises by changing vowels inside the word

steps.m: vows (object of وَلْيُوفُوا - and let them fulfill)
steps.n: Shows this word is receiving the action of fulfilling from the verb وَلْيُوفُوا in the ayah

steps.m: their vows (object of وَلْيُوفُوا - and let them fulfill)
steps.n: Adding possessive pronoun suffix هم (their)

steps.m: to circle or go around something

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive circling, like doing multiple circuits
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start, with ت merging into ط to make it stronger
Added لْ to make it a command, like saying 'let him do' something

steps.m: and let him circle intensively (connected to لْيَقْضُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous command لْيَقْضُوا۟ (let them complete)

steps.m: and let them circle intensively (around بِٱلْبَيْتِ)
steps.n: Added وا to make it plural - now it's talking about a group of people circling around the House (Ka'bah)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the place where dwelling happens
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific
Added بِ at the beginning to show how the house is involved in the action
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. This word is what they should perform tawaf around in وَلْيَطَّوَّفُوا۟ (and let them perform tawaf).

steps.m: to be old/ancient or to be free

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to create an intensive adjective meaning 'very ancient/old'
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word describes البيت (the House) which comes after the word بِ (in/by)
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something close
Added alif mark (ٰ) to show this is a variant spelling
Added لِ to change from pointing to something close to something at medium distance

steps.m: that (pointing to something not very close)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun form
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word is the subject of يُعَظِّمْ (honors/magnifies) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'became great' - fatha (َ) on first letter, damma (ُ) on middle letter
Added shadda to show that someone is actively making something else great
Added يُ at beginning to show present tense and changed middle vowel to kasra

steps.m: he honors/magnifies (حُرُمٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - the sacred things of Allah)
steps.n: Added sukun at end to show this is a conditional verb (whoever honors...). This word is connected to حُرُمٰتِ ٱللَّهِ which means 'the sacred things of Allah'

steps.m: to forbid or make something sacred

steps.n: Added damma vowels to make a plural noun showing multiple sacred things
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple sacred things or sanctities
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase حُرُمٰتِ ٱللَّهِ
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by the word حُرُمٰتِ (sacred ordinances) in the phrase 'sacred ordinances of Allah'
Combines 'then' (ف) with the pronoun 'he/it' (هو)
Changes from verb meaning to noun meaning of 'good'
Added double damma to show this is describing what something is. In this ayah, it's telling us that something is better for the person mentioned (هُوَ)
Starting with the basic particle ل that shows direction or purpose
The letter gets doubled (shadda) because it joins with the same letter from the previous word, making the meaning stronger
Adding هُ shows who the word is directed to - in this case 'him' or 'it'
The ۥ mark is added to show pronunciation elongation but doesn't change the meaning
This word shows location or closeness in relation to 'his Lord' in this ayah
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis on the middle letter. This creates the basic noun meaning 'Lord'.
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after عِندَ (meaning 'with/at') which requires the thing after it to show ownership
Added هِۦ at the end to show this Lord belongs to 'him'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from active 'he made lawful' to passive 'it was made lawful' by adding أُ at start and doubling the middle letter

steps.m: it was made lawful (referring to الْأَنْعٰمُ - livestock)
steps.n: The ت shows this is about a feminine subject (الْأَنْعٰمُ - livestock) in the past tense

steps.m: and it was made lawful (referring to الْأَنْعٰمُ - livestock)
steps.n: Adds conjunction prefix to connect with previous phrase
Added fatha to the letter ل to help with pronunciation
Added كُم which means 'you all' to show who the action is for
Added damma at the end to help connect this word with the verb أُحِلَّتْ (was made permissible) in this ayah
Root meaning 'comfort/ease' becomes a noun meaning 'livestock' - animals that provide comfort/benefits
Changes the word to mean many animals by adding أ at the start and ا after second letter. This is a common way to make plurals in Arabic.
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's the subject

This is a fixed word used to show exceptions. It connects what comes before it with what comes after it, showing that the second part is different from or not included in the first part. In this ayah, it's showing that some types of animals are exceptions to what was made permissible.

steps.m: that which (is being recited to you)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun referring to what is being recited (يُتْلَىٰ). It connects the previous statement about permissible animals with the exceptions that follow.

steps.m: to recite or follow something in sequence

steps.n: The و at the end changes to ا in past tense verbs
Adding يُ at the start and sukun on the middle letter makes it passive present tense - showing the action is being done to something rather than someone doing it
Adding ى at the end shows this is a passive verb that ends with a weak letter
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to be on the side or away from something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action to/for oneself
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is a present tense verb
Added وا at the end to change from 'he keeps away' to 'you all keep away' (command form)

steps.m: so you all keep yourselves away
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect with previous statement
Changed the root into a noun that means the actual dirt or uncleanliness itself
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific dirt. This word is what is being avoided in the command فَٱجْتَنِبُوا۟ (so avoid)
The shadda makes the meaning stronger to show this is a serious kind of spiritual dirt

steps.m: the great dirt (being acted upon by فَٱجْتَنِبُوا۟)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this dirt is what the command 'avoid' is acting on
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْأَوْثٰنِ (the idols). This is like adding a small bridge between words to make them flow better when reading.

steps.m: to set up or establish firmly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to form the singular noun meaning 'idol' - something set up for worship
Changed to broken plural form meaning multiple idols
Added 'the' to make it specific and added kasra at the end because it follows مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: to stay away from, to avoid
steps.n: Command form (imperative) telling someone to avoid something, with extra emphasis on staying away
Changed to a command for multiple people

steps.m: and you all avoid! (command to stay away from idols)
steps.n: Adds 'and' at the beginning. This word is commanding people to stay away from idols mentioned in the ayah.
Root meaning 'speak' becomes the noun 'speech' by adding the vowels a and silent w
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object (thing being avoided) in the phrase 'avoid false speech'

steps.m: to turn away from truth/right path

steps.n: Root meaning 'turning away from truth' becomes a noun for lies/false speech
The notes are incorrect - there is no doubling of letters here. The only change is the vowel mark from 'a' (فتحة) to 'u' (ضمة) which slightly intensifies the meaning
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ز because it comes after ال
Added kasra to show this word follows قَوْلَ (speech of) in the ayah

steps.m: to turn away from falsehood toward truth
one who turns away from falsehood toward truth
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality of a person

steps.m: those who turn away from falsehood toward truth (plural)
steps.n: Changed the word pattern to make it plural, showing multiple people with this quality

steps.m: those who turn away from falsehood toward truth (as direct objects)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show these people are being described or acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: for/to Allah (connected to حُنَفَآءَ - being upright)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show the meaning 'for/to' and ِ at the end to connect it with the previous word حُنَفَآءَ (being upright). Together they mean 'being upright for Allah.'

steps.m: to be different or to change

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing حُنَفَآءَ (upright ones)
Added basic vowels to make the root into a past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action

steps.m: those who join partners (with Allah) (acted upon in غَيْرَ)
steps.n: Added ين to show plural and that this word is being acted upon by غَيْرَ (meaning 'other than')
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show who/what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to اللَّهِ (Allah).
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Combines the conjunction وَ (and) with مَن (who/whoever)
This is the basic root meaning showing the concept of sharing or associating
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding sukun at end to show it's conditional (because it comes after وَمَن - 'and whoever')
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda on ل to make this the unique name for the one true God
Added fatha under the shadda for proper pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show 'with/by' and changed alif to alif wasl (ٱ) since it comes after another letter
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added comparative particle كَ which shows similarity
Added أَنَّ (anna) to make it mean 'it is as though' instead of just 'like'

steps.m: then it is as if (describing someone falling from the sky)
steps.n: Added مَا to make the comparison stronger - like saying 'exactly as if' instead of just 'as if'
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added basic past tense vowel pattern to show this happened in the past
The middle letter is doubled (shown by the ّ mark) to show the falling was intense or sudden
This is a preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to 'the sky' (ٱلسَّمَآءِ) to show falling from there.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Adds اء ending to make it a noun meaning sky/heaven
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ٱل
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from). The alif is written longer but pronounced the same.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added تَ prefix to show present tense with feminine subject (birds) and changed vowels for present tense. The birds (الطَّيْرُ) are doing the snatching.
Added هُ (him) at the end to show who is being snatched. Note: The word is not related to خَرَّ - that part was incorrect.
Added فَ (then) to show this happens after falling from the sky (خَرَّ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ)

steps.m: to fly or soar through the air
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Changed from action (flying) to thing that does the action (bird). In this ayah, the birds are doing the action of snatching.
Adding ال makes it specific birds, and the shadda on ط comes automatically after ال

steps.m: the birds (as the ones doing the action)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows these birds are doing the action of snatching in the verse

This is a conjunction showing two possible ways something can happen - either the birds snatch him or the wind throws him. In this ayah, it connects two verbs describing what happens to someone who associates partners with Allah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Root letters got vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he fell'

steps.m: it (the wind) carries/throws (something being thrown by الرِّيحُ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense feminine form because الرِّيحُ (the wind) is feminine in Arabic. The wind is the subject doing the throwing/carrying action.
Added kasra to the preposition ب to make it usable in speech
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what/who the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Changed و to ي and added kasra to make it mean the thing that blows
Added ال to specify this particular wind
Added damma to show this wind is doing the action of تَهْوِى (carries away)
This is a preposition showing where something happens. In this ayah, it shows where the wind (ٱلرِّيحُ) carries (تَهْوِى) something.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens
Added ٍ at the end because it comes after the word في (in). In this ayah, it describes a distant or far-off place where something is thrown.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show intensity of the quality of being distant

steps.m: very distant/far away (describing مَكَانٍ - a place)
steps.n: Added the genitive case ending because this word describes مَكَانٍ (place) which comes after فِى (in)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's a long vowel
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to what follows in وَمَن يُعَظِّمْ)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word, making it point to something specific that's far away. In this ayah, it points forward to the next statement about honoring Allah's symbols.
This is a question word that became a conditional particle meaning 'whoever'. In this ayah, it introduces the person who does the action in يُعَظِّمْ (honors/magnifies).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects it to the previous statement about honoring sacred things.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'became big/great'
Adding the shadda changes it from 'becoming great' to 'making something else great' - like changing from growing big to making something else big
Adding يُ at the start changes it from past to present tense. The kasra under ظ is required with this form.
The sukun at the end shows this is part of a conditional phrase - whoever magnifies...

steps.m: a ritual or symbol (something that represents something else)
steps.n: Added vowels and the letter ي to make a singular noun meaning one ritual or symbol. The ة at the end makes it refer to one specific instance.
This changes the word to mean many rituals or symbols. The word اللَّهِ after it shows these are specifically Allah's rituals/symbols.
This is the basic concept the word comes from - something that is worshipped or given divine status
Changed from action to noun - something that is worshipped
Added shadda to emphasize this is THE one true deity, not just any deity
Changed to show this belongs to or relates to something else. In this ayah, it shows that the reverence belongs to Allah.
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds emphasis to what follows
Adds فَ to show this follows from the previous statement about honoring Allah's symbols

steps.m: then indeed it (referring to honoring Allah's symbols)
steps.n: Adds هَا to refer back to 'honoring Allah's symbols' mentioned in the previous part of the verse
Basic preposition showing that something comes from or originates from something else. Here it shows that the previous action comes from تَقْوَى (piety/consciousness of Allah).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: piety, God-consciousness (quality of protecting oneself from Allah's displeasure)
steps.n: The prefix تَ and the ending ى work together to create an abstract noun meaning the state or quality of protecting oneself from what displeases Allah
The root meaning of turning becomes 'heart' because the heart is always turning and changing. This is like how in English we say our heart 'turns' toward something we love.
Changes the vowels and adds و to show we're talking about many hearts. In this ayah, these hearts belong to or contain تَقْوَى (God-consciousness).
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular hearts, and ِ to show these hearts belong to something (تَقْوَى)
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to the benefits (مَنٰفِعُ) mentioned later in the ayah, showing these benefits are for you all.
Added kasra vowel mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: in it (feminine) (referring to the sacrificial animals mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what is being referred to. The ها refers back to the sacrificial animals (which are feminine in Arabic)

steps.m: to help or give something good

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he benefited' to 'benefits' by adding مَ at the start and making it plural with ا and ِ between letters to show multiple useful things
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the main subject, and shortened the long ا with ٰ
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to أَجَلٍ (appointed term) to show 'towards an appointed term'
Added an alif marker (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound at the end
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to the deadline itself rather than the action of setting one

steps.m: an appointed term (connected to إِلَىٰٓ meaning 'until')
steps.n: The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite ('an' instead of 'the') and is being acted upon by the word إِلَىٰٓ (until) that comes before it

steps.m: something that has been named or appointed
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels turns this from 'to name' into 'something that was named'
Adding shadda on م and س makes the meaning stronger, showing this is definitely and firmly appointed
Adding tanween (ً) shows this is describing an appointed time or term mentioned in the ayah (أَجَلٍ)
Added vowel marks and shadda to complete the word. This word connects two ideas in sequence, showing that مَحِلُّهَآ (its destination) comes after the previous statement.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added مَ at start and ِ in middle to show this is a place where something happens. The last two letters combine with shadda.
Changes to show this word is doing the action (adds u-sound at end)

steps.m: its place of settling (referring to the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Added هَآ at the end to show this belongs to something feminine (the sacrificial animals mentioned earlier)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the benefits (مَنٰفِعُ) with their destination (ٱلْبَيْتِ/the House).
Added an alif mark (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
The root becomes a noun meaning the place where you spend the night
The noun form refers to a place where people dwell. This word follows the preposition إِلَى (to) in this ayah.
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra ending shows this word follows إِلَى (to). In this ayah, this refers specifically to the Ka'bah, which is described as the Ancient House (ٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْعَتِيقِ).
The root combines the concepts of age and freedom/nobility
Added vowels and ي to create an adjective describing the Ka'bah's ancient and noble status

steps.m: the Ancient House (referring to the Ka'bah)
steps.n: The definite article ال makes this a specific reference to the Ka'bah

steps.m: of the Ancient House (referring to the Ka'bah)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this describes something belonging to or connected to the House, as part of the phrase إِلَى ٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْعَتِيقِ (to the Ancient House)
The doubled ل (shadda) intensifies the meaning of totality/wholeness
The لِ prefix shows ownership or purpose
Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous statement. This word connects to أُمَّةٍ (nation/community) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to be in front, to lead as an example

steps.n: The root letters were combined with a shadda and damma to make the word for 'mother'
Adding ة changes it from 'mother' to mean a whole community of people

steps.m: a community (belonging to وَلِكُلِّ - and for every)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this community belongs to 'every' (كُلِّ) in the phrase 'and for every community'
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to create the basic past tense verb form
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. This is Allah speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'.

steps.m: to perform rituals or acts of worship

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens. This word is what was 'made/appointed' by جَعَلْنَا (we made).

steps.m: a place of ritual/worship (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what was appointed/made. It's the object that receives the action of جَعَلْنَا (we made/appointed).
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why they should do the action
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and small circle above وا to mark end of word

steps.m: to be elevated or high above

steps.n: The root meaning 'high/elevated' becomes the noun 'name'. The و at the end falls off, which commonly happens in Arabic words.
Added ٱ at beginning to help pronunciation. Changed final ُ to َ because this word is the object of يَذْكُرُوا (they remember/mention).
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added shadda to emphasize the L sound. The hamza at the start changed to a special connecting hamza (ٱ).
This is the basic preposition form showing something is on top of or over something else

steps.m: upon (something that will be mentioned after)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this word connects to what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: what (that which رَزَقَهُم - He provided them)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb رَزَقَهُم (He provided them) to show what was provided. It's like saying 'that which' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added هُم at the end to show who received the provision
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of, like saying 'from' or 'of' in English
The shadda (ّ) is added because the next word starts with ب, making the ن sound blend into it when speaking
The small ۢ mark shows that the ن blends smoothly into the ب of the next word بَهِيمَةِ (livestock/cattle)
Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show a lasting quality

steps.m: an animal (because it can't speak clearly)
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to one specific type of thing that has this quality
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to the word الْأَنْعٰمِ (livestock) that comes after it
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he lived comfortably' in the past tense
Adding أ at the beginning makes it about causing or giving the action to others
Changed from a verb meaning 'to bless' to a completely different noun meaning 'livestock' - these are separate words, not a transformation
Adds 'the' (ال) and shows the livestock belongs to someone

steps.m: to worship or take as a deity

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'a deity'
The small alif makes the 'aa' sound longer
Added ُ to show this word is the main subject being talked about
Added كُمْ at the end to show this god belongs to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: so/then your god (connected to the previous statement about remembering Allah's name)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement about remembering Allah's name when sacrificing animals

steps.m: to be devoted to, to worship

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif (ٰ) makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading

steps.m: a god (one of multiple deities being discussed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is referring to one of multiple gods being discussed in the context
Adding vowels to make it a proper word
The alif (ٰ) was added and the double-damma (ٌ) at the end turns this into a word that means 'one' as a describing word
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement
Added هُ at the end to show who is being referred to (him)
Added pronunciation helpers (ۥٓ) that don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the start to change from 'being peaceful' to 'making oneself peaceful through submission to Allah'
Changed from 'he submitted' to 'submit!' by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'i' - this makes it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The shadda doubles the middle letter to make the verb stronger and turns it into a command
Added kasra at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word (المخبتين)

steps.m: and bring good news! (command to the Prophet ﷺ about المخبتين)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command to previous statements. This is a command to the Prophet ﷺ to give good news to المخبتين (the humble ones).

steps.m: one who is humble and submissive
steps.n: Added مُ at the beginning to show 'the one who' does the action (like saying 'the humble one')
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people who are humble and submissive

steps.m: the humble and submissive ones (mentioned in وَبَشِّرِ - and give good news to)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at beginning to mean 'the' and َ (fatha) at end because it comes after وَبَشِّرِ (give good news to)
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
This is the basic form of the word that indicates a point in time

steps.m: when (introduces the condition وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ - their hearts become fearful)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes this from just meaning 'at the time' to meaning 'when' in a conditional sense. It introduces what happens when Allah is mentioned - their hearts become fearful.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that the action happened to something rather than someone doing it. This word appears in the context of 'when Allah is mentioned'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Connected the root letters with vowels to show a feeling or emotion. This word describes what happened to قُلُوبُهُمْ (their hearts) in this ayah.
Added ت to show this is about 'it' (referring to قُلُوبُهُمْ which is feminine in Arabic)

steps.m: to turn over, flip, or transform

steps.n: Changed the internal structure to make it plural by adding damma vowels and و, turning a verb root into a noun meaning 'hearts' - the organ that can turn/change direction
Added هُمْ with ُ connector to show these hearts belong to them
Adding an alif after the first letter and kasra after the second letter changes this from an action to describing someone who does that action
The ين ending shows this is plural masculine in the accusative case. It is the object (thing being acted upon) in the sentence.
Adding ال makes it specific/definite - now talking about particular patient people
Adding وَ (wa) connects this word to what came before it, meaning 'and'
Adding ٱل makes the word definite, meaning 'the patient ones' instead of just any patient ones
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of another. Here it connects with 'what befell them' in the ayah.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Connected the basic letters of this relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'
Changed the alif to a long alif (آ). This is just a writing style change and doesn't affect the meaning. In this ayah, it refers to whatever hardships or trials struck them.
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا.
Added 'أ' at the start changes 'hit' into 'caused to hit/afflict' - like turning 'hit' into 'strike down'

steps.m: it afflicted/befell them (referring to what happened to قُلُوبُهُمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: The هُمْ shows this happened to multiple people. This word relates to قُلُوبُهُمْ (their hearts) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to establish, set up, make firm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُ at start and changed middle letter و to ي to show who does the action
Added و at start to mean 'and'

steps.m: and those who establish (connected to ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ - the prayer)
steps.n: Added ال (the) and ى to change from singular to plural - means 'those who' instead of 'one who'
Basic root letters for 'pray' got vowels to make 'he prayed' in past tense
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a noun for a single instance of prayer
Added kasra to show possession or connection
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and wrote prayer in the special Quran spelling with وٰ
The letter ن in مِن assimilates into the م of ما, shown by the shadda

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمْ to show who received the provision. The alif in نَا becomes ٰ when connected to هُمْ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action in present time
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Changes the word into a noun describing physical bodies/forms
Added ال to specify these particular sacrificial camels

steps.m: and the large-bodied camels (object of جَعَلْنٰهَا)
steps.n: Added 'and' at beginning and fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلْنٰهَا (We made them)

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the last vowel to sukun to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نَا to show 'we' did this action
Added هَا to show what was made/set (referring back to الْبُدْنَ - the sacrificial camels mentioned before)
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam
Added the pronoun كم which means 'you all' to show who the preposition is directed to
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending with ن or similar sounds. This is just for smooth pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to mark or sense something important

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Added ي and ة to change from 'sensing' to mean 'one religious ritual/symbol'
Changed the word to show many rituals instead of just one
Changed to show these rituals belong to Allah (possession)
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining it in a unique way
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness
Added kasra at the end to show possession and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows purpose or direction

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلْنٰهَا - we made it)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كم to show who the preposition is directed to. This word is connected to جَعَلْنٰهَا (we made it) showing who it was made for.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows where something is located.

steps.m: in it/her (referring to the sacrificial animal mentioned as البُدْن)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the sacrificial animal (البُدْن) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This connects this word to what was discussed before.
Changes from root letters into a noun meaning 'good/goodness'
Added double-damma ending to show this is the main subject doing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he remembered' to 'remember!' by adding 'u' at start to make it a command
Adding وا at the end makes it a command for many people instead of one person
Added the conjunction فَ (fa) at the beginning to indicate 'so/then'
Changes from verb 'to name' into the noun 'a name'
The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of remembering/mentioning. The ٱ is a different way of writing اِ at the start of words.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on lam, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This shows that the 'signs' belong to Allah.
This is a basic preposition showing position or relationship. It doesn't come from a root.
Added ي with sukun (ْ) as a connecting letter to prepare for the next suffix

steps.m: on/upon her/it (referring to the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Added ها to show this refers to the sacrificial animals (البُدْن) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to line up or arrange in rows

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: one who is standing in a row
steps.n: Changed from a verb (doing something) to describe someone who is doing it

steps.m: multiple beings standing in rows (describes the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Changed to plural form to describe multiple sacrificial animals standing in rows for ritual slaughter
This is a basic particle meaning 'when' that indicates something happening in the future or as a condition

steps.m: then when (connects to وَجَبَتْ - they fall down)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to what happens when the sacrificial animals fall down.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: it (feminine) fell down (referring to جُنُوبُهَا - their sides)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show this is about something feminine (the sides of the sacrificial animals) falling down after slaughter
Forms the basic noun for 'side' by adding vowel patterns
Makes the word plural by changing the vowels and adding و in the middle
Adds feminine possessive pronoun suffix هَا. This word is the subject doing the action in وَجَبَتْ (fell down)
The وا suffix makes it plural masculine, addressing multiple people
Changes root 'eat' into command for group - 'all of you eat!'
Adding the conjunction prefix فَ to indicate sequence/result
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it's used to show what you should eat from (the sacrificial animals).

steps.m: from it/her (referring to the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to the sacrificial animals (البُدْن) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic root meaning about providing food or nourishment
Added vowels to make it 'he fed' in the past tense
Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'to cause someone to do the action'

steps.m: you all feed! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding وا makes it a command for a group of people
Added و at beginning to connect this command with previous commands in the verse

steps.m: to be satisfied with what one has
one who is satisfied with what they receive (describes someone who doesn't beg)
steps.n: Added alif after first root letter and vowels to show this describes someone who has this quality. In this ayah, it refers to poor people who are content with whatever they receive without asking.

steps.m: the content poor person (who doesn't beg)
steps.n: Adding ال and the ending َ makes it specific and shows it's being acted upon (receiving meat) in this ayah

steps.m: to approach or come seeking something

steps.n: Added shadda to show repeated action
Changed from a verb to a noun describing someone who asks others for help persistently
Added the definite article to specify this particular person

steps.m: and the one who approaches seeking (help)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect with previous word (ٱلْقَانِعَ) and final vowel mark

steps.m: and the one who persistently asks/approaches
steps.n: Adding ال makes the word definite, meaning 'the' specific one who asks
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذٰ (that) to specify what is being compared to
Added لِ to complete the word كَذٰلِكَ meaning 'like that' - this is just part of the full word

steps.m: like that (addressing someone) (connected to سَخَّرْنٰهَا - We have subjected them)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is being addressed to someone. This word connects to سَخَّرْنٰهَا (We have subjected them) in the next part of the ayah, showing how something was done.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled خ shows the action was done thoroughly or completely
Changed the subject from 'he' to 'we' by adding نا at the end. The ْ (sukun) is added before نا to help pronunciation.
Added ها to show what was made to serve (referring back to the sacrificial animals mentioned earlier in the verse). The alif in نا becomes longer (ٰ) when ها is added.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for you all (connected to خَيْرٌ - good)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being addressed - multiple people. This word connects to خَيْرٌ (good) in the ayah to show that the good/benefit is for them.
This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation about something

steps.m: so that you all might (connected to تَشْكُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to a group of people being addressed. This connects to the next word تَشْكُرُونَ (you give thanks) to complete the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show it's happening now

steps.m: you all thank (referring to the people being addressed in this verse)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed
This is a negative particle that makes future actions impossible
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle letter ي becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. In this ayah, it comes after لَن which means 'will not', so together they mean 'will not reach'.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being magnified in the phrase 'so that you may magnify Allah'
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun
Changed to broken plural by adding و and changing vowels to damma (u sound)

steps.m: its meats (referring to the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Added ها to show possession and ُ before it to connect the sounds
Starting with the basic negation particle that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous statement about لُحُومُهَا (their meat)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form the base word
Added hamza at the end to make it plural. This is a special plural form for words ending in weak letters.
Changed the spelling to accommodate the upcoming pronoun. The alif becomes long (آ) and hamza gets a damma (ؤُ)

steps.m: its/her bloods (referring to the sacrificial animals)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show possession - referring back to the sacrificial animals mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic word that shows contrast between ideas. The kasra is added to help with pronunciation.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous statement. This shows that what follows contrasts with what came before about the meat and blood not reaching Allah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb. The middle letter ي combines with َ to form ا.
Changes from past tense (he obtained) to present tense (he obtains) by adding يَ at the start
Adds هُ meaning 'it' referring to righteousness (التَّقْوَىٰ) mentioned later in the ayah

steps.m: to protect oneself or others from harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: protecting oneself (through being mindful of God)
steps.n: Changed from 'he protected' to mean 'protecting oneself' by adding ت at start and ى at end - this makes it a special type of noun that means the action itself

steps.m: the protecting oneself (through being mindful of God)
steps.n: Added ال to make it 'the taqwa' - this makes it specific/definite
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all' to show who is being referred to
Starting with the demonstrative pronoun 'this/that'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this word.
Added لِ which means 'to/for' to show purpose or direction
Added كَ at the end which shows this is being addressed to you
Added كَ at the beginning to mean 'like' or 'similar to', making the complete meaning 'like that' or 'likewise'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added shadda makes it mean 'to make something/someone do the action' instead of doing it yourself

steps.m: he made it/her serve a purpose (referring to the sacrificial animals mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ها ('it/her') at the end to show which specific thing was made to serve - referring back to the sacrificial animals
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُم to show who the action is for. The ْ (sukun) at the end shows this is the end of the word.
Adding a shadda (double letter) in the middle makes the action stronger - like really magnify instead of just magnify
Adding تُ changes it from command to present tense 'you'
The ُ at the end shows this is happening in the present
Added plural masculine subject marker وا to indicate plural 'you'
Added لِ prefix to show purpose ('so that/in order to') and small high rounded zero (۟) at end
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
The small alif makes the 'aa' sound longer
Added 'the' to make it definite
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
This is the base form of the preposition that shows something being in a higher position
Adding ى at the end makes it work as a preposition showing position or relationship
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show the long 'aa' sound clearly. This word connects to هَدَىٰكُمْ (guided you) to show what you are upon/following.

steps.m: that which (refers to the guidance mentioned after in هَدَىٰكُمْ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links to the guidance mentioned right after it. It helps explain why Allah made these animals available for sacrifice.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates basic past tense verb meaning 'guided'
Added كُمْ at the end to show who was guided - in this case, all of you
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows doing the action with more emphasis
Added و at beginning to connect this command with what came before

steps.m: and give good news! (to المحسنين - those who do good)
to do good, to be excellent

steps.n: The مُ prefix with the middle letter vowel ِ creates a word for someone who actively does the action

steps.m: those who do good deeds (object of بَشِّرِ)
steps.n: Changed to plural form with ين. This word is receiving the action of 'give glad tidings' (بَشِّرِ) in this ayah.

steps.m: the ones who do good deeds (object of بَشِّرِ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to specific good-doers rather than any good-doers. The word is still receiving the action of 'give glad tidings' (بَشِّرِ).
This is a basic emphasis word that has no root. It's used to make what comes after it stronger or more certain. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that Allah is the one who defends.
Added the kasra (ِ) under the first letter and shadda (ّ) on the second letter to show how it's pronounced. The shadda makes the ن sound stronger.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end because this word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily). The hamza at the start is written differently (ٱ) but pronounced the same.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adding alif between first and second root letters shows the action is done with more intensity or repetition

steps.m: he defends repeatedly/intensively (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with يُ prefix and added ِ (kasra) before final ُ to show it's happening now. This is what Allah does, as indicated by إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ (Indeed, Allah)
This is the basic preposition form showing movement away from something or talking about something
Added vowel marks to show how the word connects to the next word (ٱلَّذِينَ). The kasra at the end helps connect it smoothly to the next word.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and modifying it to create the relative pronoun form
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word is connected to ءَامَنُوٓا۟ (they believed) which comes right after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to change meaning from 'trust' to 'believe/have faith in'
Added plural ending to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of يُدٰفِعُ (defends) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that adds emphasis
The shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis stronger, while the kasra (ِ) shows this word affects what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the word اللَّهَ (Allah).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed the initial alif to a wasl form since it connects to the previous word.
This is a negative word that makes the next word (يُحِبُّ - He loves) mean the opposite - 'He does not love'
Added fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root share
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Doubling of middle root letter indicates intensity/emphasis of the action
Adds يُ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, and changes middle vowel to ِ
Added damma at the end to show this is the main subject of the sentence. This word follows لَا (not) in the verse.
This root relates to completeness or totality
The shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter to add emphasis, making it mean 'all/every'
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُحِبُّ (loves) in the ayah
This root is about breaking trust or being disloyal
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Doubling the middle letter shows this happens again and again
Adding alif after second root letter and removing the last vowel changes this from an action to describing someone who does this action often

steps.m: a frequent betrayer (object of لَا يُحِبُّ - does not love)
steps.n: The tanween kasra (two slanted lines under) shows this is the object of the verb 'does not love' in this ayah
This root relates to covering or hiding something, especially truth or blessings
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who does this action intensely or repeatedly

steps.m: a constantly ungrateful one (object of يُحِبُّ/loves)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُحِبُّ (loves) in لَا يُحِبُّ (does not love)
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: it was permitted (subject of يُقٰتَلُونَ - they are being fought)
steps.n: Changed the vowel on the first letter to show that the permission was given to someone rather than someone giving permission. This word is connected to يُقٰتَلُونَ (they are being fought) in the ayah, showing who received permission.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes this from pointing at something to describing someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ shows this is for or to someone. The لِ combines with ال to make لّ with a shadda. This word connects to أُذِنَ (permission was given) to show who received permission.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb
The alif shows two people/groups fighting each other
Changed from past tense 'he fought' to present passive 'being fought against' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they are being fought against (subject of أُذِنَ)
steps.n: Added ون to show plural masculine subjects. This word is the subject of أُذِنَ (permission was given).
This is a particle that introduces a reason or explanation
Added هُمْ to show who is being referred to
The prefix بِ changes the meaning from 'that they' to 'because they'

steps.m: to do wrong or harm to someone unfairly

steps.n: Changed vowels to show this is happening TO someone instead of someone doing it. This is called passive voice - like changing 'he hit' to 'was hit'.

steps.m: they were wronged/harmed unfairly (subject of أُذِنَ - permission was given)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this happened to many people. This word describes who was given permission (أُذِنَ) in this verse.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the statement that follows it. Here it emphasizes that Allah is truly capable.
Adding conjunction prefix وَ (and) to connect with previous statement
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One God
Added a fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in this sentence. The hamza at the start is written as ٱ when connecting to previous words.

steps.m: upon/on/over (connects نَصْر with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. Here it connects the word نَصْر (help/victory) to what comes before it.

steps.m: upon/on/over (connects نَصْر with what comes before)
steps.n: Added an alif mark (ٰ) after the ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Changed to a noun form meaning the act of helping

steps.m: their help/victory (what Allah is capable of giving them)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This refers to the help/victory that Allah is capable of giving to those who were wronged, as mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: to have power or ability over something

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters and kasra on middle letter to show this is a quality or characteristic

steps.m: surely having complete power (describes Allah's ability in وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis, working with إِنَّ to show strong certainty

steps.m: surely having complete power (describes Allah's ability in وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added ٌ (tanween damma) at the end because this word completes the description after إِنَّ, telling us what quality Allah has
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال with a shadda on the ل to make it specific
Added ن at the end to make it plural. This word refers to the people who were driven out of their homes, as mentioned in the next word أُخْرِجُوا۟ (they were driven out)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes someone cause the action instead of doing it themselves
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: they were made to exit (referring to الَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show this happened to a group of people. This word refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who)
Basic form of the preposition without any vowel marks
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: to move around or dwell in a place

steps.n: The root letters د-و-ر became دَار (home) by changing و to ا, showing where someone stays/lives
