Changes the word to show many homes by adding ي after first letter and changing vowels
The َ and ا combine into one long ٰ sound
Adds هِم to show the homes belong to them. This word is what they were driven out from (after مِن)

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Changes from root to noun meaning 'other than'
Added kasra at the end because it's possessed by the word حَقٍّ (right/justice) that comes after it
Added the preposition بِ to show how they were driven out - without right/justice. This phrase describes how the action أُخْرِجُوا۟ (they were driven out) happened.
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'

steps.m: any right/truth (being acted upon by بِغَيْرِ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word بِغَيْرِ (without) that comes before it
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This is a common particle used to make exceptions in Arabic.
Added the long alif (آ) at the end. This is connected to أَن يَقُولُوا۟ (that they say) which follows it in the ayah, showing what is being excepted.
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what the people say (يَقُولُوا۟). Think of it like quotation marks in English before someone speaks.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: Lord (as the doer of an action)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to nurture) into a noun meaning 'Lord' - the one who nurtures. The shaddah (double letter) shows emphasis.

steps.m: our Lord (the one being called upon)
steps.n: The suffix نا means 'our', showing we are addressing our Lord. This word is what's being said when they say (يقولوا) 'our Lord'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific

steps.m: if not for (introduces what prevents دَفْعُ from happening)
steps.n: This is a special word that introduces a condition - it shows that something would have happened if not for something else. Here it connects to دَفْعُ (repelling) in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: and if not for (connects to previous ayah about those who were driven out)
steps.n: The وَ connects this condition to the previous ayah about the people who were driven from their homes, showing how these ideas are related
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the act itself. This word follows لَوْلَا (if not for) in the ayah, showing what would be missing without Allah's intervention.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, which is a special feature of this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes in the middle of speech

steps.m: people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: The root أنس becomes نَاس by dropping the first letter (أ) and adding a long ā sound after ن to make a word meaning 'people' - those who live together in society
Adding ٱل makes it refer to specific people. The ن gets a shadda to help pronunciation when connecting with ٱل

steps.m: the people (being acted upon by دَفْعُ اللَّهِ - Allah's checking)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of Allah's checking (دَفْعُ اللَّهِ) in this ayah
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by another word
Added هُم meaning 'them' (referring to people)

steps.m: to divide/separate into parts, some, portion

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'some' or 'a portion'. This refers to some of the people (ٱلنَّاسَ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added the preposition 'bi' to show this is done 'by means of' something
Added the genitive case ending because it follows the preposition bi-
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and added shadda to show something received the action rather than did it
Added ت to show this refers to feminine things being demolished (like buildings)
Added emphatic لَّ with shadda to show this definitely would have happened

steps.m: to rise up high or tower above

steps.n: Forms a noun for a tall building used by monks for worship and meditation
Changes the word to plural form to show multiple monastery towers

steps.m: monastery towers (subject of would have been demolished)
steps.n: Adds U ending to show this word is the subject of لَّهُدِّمَتْ (would have been demolished). Also combines alif and fatha into long alif for writing style.
Changes the singular 'sale' into a plural for a small number of transactions
Adds 'and' to connect this word with previous words
Adds tanween dammah to make it indefinite

steps.m: to pray, worship, or bow down
a prayer or place of prayer
steps.n: Changed root into a noun showing either the act of prayer or place where prayer happens
The word changed from singular (one prayer) to plural (many prayers) by adding وات at the end
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite (any prayers, not specific ones)

steps.m: and places of worship/synagogues (connected to صَوٰمِعُ وَبِيَعٌ - monasteries and churches)
steps.n: Added conjunction و to connect this with the previous places of worship mentioned (monasteries and churches)
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens, like how 'kitchen' is a place for cooking
Changes to broken plural form for places/locations by adding ا after first letter
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject of would be destroyed (لَّهُدِّمَتْ) in this ayah

steps.m: and mosques (subject of would be destroyed/لَّهُدِّمَتْ)
steps.n: Added conjunction و meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it passive (something being done to it)

Changed to present tense passive by adding يُ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and adding ُ at the end. This describes what happens in the places of worship - Allah's name is mentioned there.
This is a preposition meaning 'in/within'. It's used to show location or containment.
Added هَا which refers back to the mosques (مَسٰجِدُ) mentioned earlier. This shows that Allah's name is mentioned in these mosques.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

Changed from verb 'to be high' into noun 'name' by adding alif at start and changing middle/end letters. The word 'name' comes from the idea of something being elevated or raised in status by being identified.
The alif at the start joins with the word after it, and the ending changes to show it connects to 'Allah' coming next
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: of Allah (possessive in relation to اسْمُ meaning 'name')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added shadda on ل. This word is connected to اسْمُ (name) in the phrase 'name of Allah'.
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective showing a strong quality

steps.m: abundantly/much (describes how often يُذْكَرُ Allah's name is mentioned)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word describes how the action happens. It tells us Allah's name is mentioned many times in these places.

steps.m: to help, assist, give victory to

steps.n: Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ prefix and changing vowels
Added لَ prefix to show strong emphasis about the future action
Added double emphasis with نَّ ending, showing absolute certainty

steps.m: and he will most certainly help (them)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this promise with the previous description of those who were driven from their homes. The 'them' refers to those people.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), and the shadda emphasizes the ل. The ُ shows this is the subject of يَقُولُوا (they say) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces the person who helps/supports (يَنصُرُ) Allah's cause, making it mean 'whoever helps'.

steps.m: to help, aid, or give victory to

steps.n: Adds هُ meaning 'him' as the object receiving the help, with ۥٓ showing elongation in recitation

steps.m: indeed (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is strong and mighty)
steps.n: This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It also causes the next word (اللَّهَ) to end in َ instead of ُ. It's like adding 'truly' or 'definitely' at the start of a sentence in English.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters with shadda. This word specifically refers to the One True God in Islam.
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being helped in this verse (connected to يَنصُرُ meaning 'he helps/supports')
Added vowels and doubled the final letter to make it an adjective meaning 'having strength'
Added double dammah to make it indefinite (a strong one)
Added emphatic لَ showing certainty
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ي in the middle and changing the vowels makes this describe someone or something that has a lot of strength or power
The double mark (tanween) at the end shows this word stands alone as a complete description of Allah's might
The definite article's ل assimilates with the following ذ creating shadda. This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people.

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition in this ayah. It shows that what follows (their establishment in the land) leads to certain actions (establishing prayer, giving charity, etc.). It's like saying 'if/when we establish them in the land, they do these things.'
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action
Added هُمْ (them) at the end to show who received the power, and merged two letters at start
Basic particle showing location or being inside something
The ى extends the sound and helps connect to the next word. In this ayah, it shows location within the earth/land.
Basic noun with vowel marks added - fatha (a) on first letter, sukoon (no vowel) on middle letter
The ِ shows this word follows the preposition فِى (in) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb.
Added أَ at the beginning to make the action causative - showing someone making something happen rather than doing it themselves
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action. This word describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) did when given power.

steps.m: to pray or connect through worship

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha) to each root letter to make past tense 'he prayed'
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a noun for a single prayer
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
Added ٱل to make it specific
Added ending vowel to show this is the object of the verb أَقَامُوا (they established)
The long alif makes the meaning more intense - like giving something with effort
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action

steps.m: to purify or make something grow

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show the act itself rather than someone doing it. Added ة to make it a specific religious duty.
Added ٱل to show this is the specific religious duty of giving charity. This word is what is being given in the phrase 'they gave charity'.
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being given. Changed alif to alif with bar for Quranic spelling.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make it past tense
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and they commanded (connected to أَقَامُوا and ءَاتَوُا)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

Added the definite article to specify this particular good
Added preposition بِ to show how something is done

steps.m: with/by the known good (object of وَأَمَرُوا۟)

steps.m: and they stopped (something wrong) (connected to أَمَرُوا۟)

This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. It's like saying 'from' or 'away from' in English.
Added kasra at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُنكَرِ (the wrong). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added 'mu' at start and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'something that is rejected'
Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: of the rejected/evil thing (object of عَنِ)

steps.m: and to/for Allah (connected to عٰقِبَةُ - the end result)

Changes from basic root to a noun meaning 'the result/outcome'
Adds a damma (ُ) to show this word is the subject or in nominative case
Adds the feminine ending ة to make the word refer to a feminine concept
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural
Added ال to make it definite (specific matters) and ِ at the end because it comes after عَنِ (from/concerning)

steps.m: if (introduces condition about يُكَذِّبُوكَ - they deny you)

steps.m: and if (introduces condition about يُكَذِّبُوكَ - they deny you)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what came before it, showing this is an additional scenario being discussed

steps.m: to deny truth, to reject as false
to strongly deny or reject as false
steps.n: Adding the shadda makes the denial stronger or more emphatic
The ي (ya) at the start changes it from 'denied' to 'he denies' (present time), while keeping the strong/intensive meaning
The و shows that many people (they) are doing the action
Adds direct object pronoun 'you' to complete the meaning
This is an emphasis particle that makes the verb كَذَّبَتْ (denied/rejected) that follows it more certain or definite
The فَ shows this is happening as a result of what came before (if they deny you). It connects this statement to the previous condition.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false

steps.n: The shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter to show the action is done with intensity or repeatedly. This word connects to قَبْلَهُمْ (before them) in the ayah.

Adding ت at the end shows a feminine subject (she/it) did the action in the past. Here it refers to each nation (which is feminine in Arabic) mentioned after this word: قَوْمُ نُوحٍ (people of Noah), عَادٌ (Ad), and ثَمُودُ (Thamud).

steps.m: to be in front of or before

steps.n: Changes from verb root to noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before them (referring to the people who denied the message)

steps.m: people, folk, nation (subject of كَذَّبَتْ - denied/rejected)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows this word is doing the action in the sentence. It is the subject (doer) of the verb كَذَّبَتْ which means 'denied/rejected'.
This is a proper noun (name)
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word belongs to or is connected to قَوْمُ (people), making the phrase 'people of Noah'
This is a proper noun naming an ancient Arab tribe mentioned in the Quran
Added vowels and tanween damma to make 'Aad' (the tribe's name) into the doer/subject
Added tanween dammah at the end to make it indefinite

steps.m: Thamud (an ancient Arab tribe that rejected their prophet Salih)
steps.n: This is the name of one of the ancient Arab tribes mentioned in the Quran. They were known for carving houses in mountains and for rejecting their prophet Salih.
The ُ ending shows that Thamud is doing the action of rejecting (كَذَّبَتْ) their messenger, just like the people of Noah and 'Ad did with their messengers.
The وَ connects Thamud to the other nations mentioned (people of Noah and 'Ad) who all did the same action of rejecting their messengers.
Connected the root letters together. The middle letter و becomes silent with a sukun.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of what follows in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before it in the verse
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a three-letter root. It's the name of Prophet Abraham, who is mentioned here in relation to his people (قَوْمُ means 'people of').
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make the noun form meaning 'people'
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject of what follows in the sentence
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to what came before it, like saying 'and'
This is a proper name that comes from connecting the letters with damma on the first letter

steps.m: Lut's (people) (object of وَقَوْمُ - and the people of)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is being possessed by or connected to something else. Here it's connected to 'the people of' (وَقَوْمُ) from earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changed from 'he was a companion' to mean 'companions' by rearranging the letters and adding new vowels, instead of just adding -un or -een at the end like regular plurals
This word is connected to مَدْيَنَ (Madyan) in the ayah, meaning 'and the companions of Madyan'
Basic vowels (fatha and sukun) added to make the root letters 'm-d-n' readable as 'mad-n'
Added ي to create the specific place name Madyan, which was an ancient settlement mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: Madyan (the object being referred to in this ayah)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added basic vowels to make it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone/something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: and was strongly denied/rejected (connected to previous prophets' stories)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the stories of previous prophets being rejected
This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew with no Arabic root derivation. The final alif maqsurah (ى) is part of the name's structure.
Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation: damma (ُ) on م and fatha (َ) on س. The small alif (ٰ) after ى is added to indicate the long 'aa' sound at the end.
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The final و changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to have time/respite
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. The ى changes to ي with sukun before the suffix.

steps.m: So I gave respite (to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this action happened as a result of what came before. This word relates to لِلْكٰفِرِينَ (to the disbelievers) which comes after it.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Added ا after first letter and vowels to show someone who does the action of rejecting/denying truth
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people who reject truth
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific disbelievers mentioned in the story
Added لِ at beginning to show these disbelievers are receiving the action (being given time) in the story
This is the basic form of the word that connects events happening one after another with a time gap between them

steps.m: then (connects this to وَكُذِّبَ مُوسَىٰ - and Moses was denied)
steps.n: Adds dots above and below to show it means 'then' and connects events in order - like saying 'first this happened, then that happened'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action, and changed the last fatha to sukun
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was taken (them)
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable

steps.m: so how (interrogative connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect this question to the previous statement, showing a logical sequence

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Added basic past tense pattern with fatha vowels
Changed middle root letter و to ا because it's a hollow verb
Forms a noun showing strong or intense denial. This word follows كَانَ (was) in the ayah.

Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (فَكَأَيِّن). This helps connect the words smoothly when speaking.

steps.m: to gather or settle in a place

steps.n: Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make a noun meaning 'gathering'

steps.m: a town/village (a place where people gather)
steps.n: Added ة to make it a specific place noun
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object being destroyed in the phrase أَهْلَكْنٰهَا (We destroyed it)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes the meaning from 'to perish' to 'to cause to perish/destroy'
نا means 'we' when attached to verbs. This is a past tense verb with the subject pronoun 'we' attached to it.

steps.m: we destroyed it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ - town)
steps.n: ها refers back to قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a pronoun that refers to the feminine noun قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The kasra (ِ) under ه and alif (ى) with fatha (َ) show this is feminine.
Adding وَ (wa) at the start connects this word to what came before it, showing this is another description of the town
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
This pattern shows who does the action. Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to make it into a doer.
Added ة at the end to make it refer to a female doer of the action
Added tanween dammah to show this is describing the state of the town. This word is telling us what state the town was in when Allah destroyed it.
This is the basic feminine pronoun used to refer back to قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects to the previous part about the destroyed town.

steps.m: being empty/hollow (describes the state of عُرُوشِهَا - its trellises)

steps.m: lying empty/hollow (feminine, describes the destroyed town)
steps.n: Added ٌ (tanween damma) to show this is a describing word that tells us how the town looked
This is a primitive preposition that is not derived from any root. It is one of the core prepositions in Arabic.
Added fatha marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's an alif maksura. This word connects to عُرُوشِهَا (its trellises) to tell us what is on/upon something.

steps.m: to build or raise a structure

steps.n: Forms the basic noun for a raised structure or throne
Changes the word to plural form showing multiple structures
Adds kasra to show this word is possessed by something and follows the word عَلَىٰ (upon)
Adds feminine possessive pronoun referring back to قَرْيَةٍ (town/city) which is feminine
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the hole dug for water
Adding tanwin (two kasras) makes it mean 'a well' instead of 'the well'
Added 'and' to connect with previous phrase. From context, this refers to an abandoned well.
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of complete abandonment
Added مُ at the start to change from 'he abandoned' (a verb) to 'abandoned' (describing something)
Added ة to show this describes a feminine thing (like a well or city)
Added tanween kasra to show this word is in the genitive case (after a preposition)
The م has a shaddah added to merge with the previous word, but the word still means abandoned/neglected (describing a well)

steps.m: to confine or make something into a limited space

steps.n: Root letters were given vowels to make the word mean 'palace' - a special building that keeps people safe inside
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show it's indefinite (like saying 'a palace' instead of 'the palace')
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this word to the previous one (بِئْرٍ - well)
Added shadda to show intense building action
Changed from a verb (he built) to describe something that was built tall and high
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - emphasizes how firmly it was built
Added tanwin to show this describes the palace (قَصْر) mentioned in the ayah
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
The فَ shows this question follows from what came before it

steps.m: And did they not? (before يَسِيرُوا۟ - travel)
steps.n: The لَمْ makes the question negative and connects to the next verb يَسِيرُوا۟ (travel). Together they ask 'Have they not traveled?'

steps.m: to move or travel along a path

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'travel' to a noun meaning 'the act of traveling' by adding vowels
Adding ي at start shows this is happening now (present tense)
Adding َ and ِ marks shows this is a normal present tense verb

steps.m: they travel (in response to أَفَلَمْ - 'then have they not')
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb is part of a question starting with أَفَلَمْ (then have they not).
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It has no root since it's a primitive word.
Added the elongated ى at the end. In this ayah, it shows location within 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ).
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition فِى (in/on) in the ayah
Added ت at beginning to show present tense, and ُ (damma) on ك to show it's happening now. This word follows فَ (then) in the ayah.
Added فَ at beginning to show this happens after something else. In this ayah, it connects to the previous action of traveling through the land.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of this preposition
Added ه (hu) meaning 'him' to show who the word 'for' refers to
Added م with sukun to change the pronoun from singular (him) to plural (them)
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the singular noun 'heart'. A heart is named this way because it constantly turns blood over in the body.
Changed the internal vowel pattern and added و to make it plural. This is called a 'broken plural' because it breaks up the original word pattern instead of just adding an ending.
Added double damma (tanween) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action of understanding (يَعْقِلُونَ) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they use their minds (referring to those who don't reflect on Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who don't use their minds to reflect on Allah's signs in creation.
Basic preposition meaning 'with'

steps.m: with it (referring to ears that hear)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is being referred to (the ears mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: with it (referring to ears that hear)
steps.n: Extended the alif at the end for pronunciation emphasis, but doesn't change the meaning

This is a connecting word (particle) that links two options together. Here it connects 'hearts' (قُلُوبٌ) with 'ears' (ءَاذَانٌ) in the ayah, asking if people have either hearts to understand or ears to hear.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'ear' by adding damma vowels
Changes the word to show many ears instead of just one
Adds tanween to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changes from past tense (he heard) to present tense (he hears) by adding يَ at the start
Adds masculine plural subject marker to indicate multiple doers
Starting with the preposition بِ which shows how something is done
Added هَا which refers back to the hearts (قُلُوب) mentioned earlier in the verse. This connects the preposition to what it's talking about.
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds emphasis to statements
Adding هَا suffix for feminine singular pronoun
Adding فَ prefix to show this is a result of what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about traveling through the land.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَعْمَى (become blind) into 'do not become blind'. It shows that something is NOT happening.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
ت at start shows this is happening now (present tense)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic noun form
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple sights/visions

steps.m: the eyesights (subject of تَعْمَى - become blind)
steps.n: Added the definite article 'the' and nominative case ending because this word is the subject of تَعْمَى (become blind) in this ayah
This is a basic particle that shows contrast between ideas, like saying 'but' in English. It's used to introduce an opposite or different idea from what came before.

steps.m: and yet/but still (contrasts with previous statement فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَعْمَى ٱلْأَبْصٰرُ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement. This combination is very common in Arabic to show a strong contrast, like saying 'and yet' or 'but still' in English.
Added vowels (fatha, kasra, fatha) to make it mean 'he became blind' in the past
Added ت at start to make it 'she' form, with vowels for present tense

steps.m: to turn/change (especially about hearts and emotions)

steps.n: Adding these vowels changes the word from a verb into a noun meaning 'heart'
Changes the internal vowels and adds و to make it plural

steps.m: the hearts (subject of تَعْمَى - becomes blind)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject doing the action of becoming blind
The ل in ال assimilates into the following ت creating a shadda (solar letter assimilation)
This is a preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it shows where the people should travel (in/within the earth).

steps.m: chest, breast (where things come from inside)
steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the noun meaning 'chest' - where things come from inside
Changed the vowels from a-a to u-u and added و to make it plural (more than one chest)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ص because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the chests/breasts (that are in them)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word follows فِي (in) from the ayah
Root letters got vowel marks (fat-ha) to make past tense 'he rushed'
Adding 'است' at the beginning shows someone is actively trying to make the rushing happen
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added ك to show who they are rushing (you)

steps.m: and they seek to rush you (with بِٱلْعَذَابِ - with the punishment)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous statement. The word بِٱلْعَذَابِ that follows shows what they are trying to rush.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added long ا after second root letter to make it a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added preposition ب to show how they are hastening. This word is what they are hastening for in يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (they hasten)

1. Changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is how it's written in the Quran
2. Added a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after the word بِ (meaning 'with')
This is a particle that makes something definitely not happen in the future. Here it negates the verb يُخْلِفَ (break/fail), showing Allah will never break His promise.
Adds coordinating conjunction while preserving the negative future meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb

steps.m: he breaks/violates (the subject of لَن meaning 'will never')
steps.n: The word لَن before this verb means 'will never', so together they mean 'He (Allah) will never break'. This is about Allah's promise (وَعْدَهُۥ).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific

steps.m: to make a commitment or promise

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'promise'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُخْلِفَ (break/fail)
Added هُ (meaning 'His') with small و above in Quranic style
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. It makes the statement stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it in the ayah
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form

steps.m: a day (object of يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ - they seek to hasten)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being hastened in the phrase. This connects to the verb يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (they seek to hasten) in the ayah.
Added vowels to make it into a location word. This word shows where something is in relation to رَبِّكَ (your Lord) in this ayah.
Added fatha and shadda to create an intensive doer noun showing someone who nurtures completely
Added kasra to show this word is owned by what comes after it
Added كَ to show it belongs to 'you'. This word is possessed by عِندَ (with/at) in the ayah.
The root letters were given vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make the number 'thousand'. This is a common number word in Arabic.
Added the prefix كَ which means 'like' or 'similar to'
Added the genitive case ending ِ because this word is part of a possession relationship with سَنَةٍ (years), forming the phrase 'like a thousand years'
Connected the first two root letters with fatha vowels

steps.m: a single year (one complete cycle of time)
steps.n: Adds ة at the end to make it mean 'one year' instead of just 'passing time'

steps.m: a year (possessed by something mentioned after it)
steps.n: Adds a mark at the end to show this year belongs to or is connected to something after it
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added ما which means 'what' to specify what we're talking about
The ن in مِنْ combines with the م in ما, shown by the shadda (ّ). This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he counted' to a command 'count!' by changing vowels to u-u and adding shadda
Added تَ at the beginning to show who is doing the counting
Added ونَ at the end to show that many people are doing the counting
This is a compound word combining كَ (like) and أَيّ (which) that evolved to mean 'how many'. The shadda shows emphasis on the ي.
Added ن at the end with kasra under the ي to complete this special expression.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it.
Basic preposition form without any vowels
Added kasra to show it's a preposition

Added shadda because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (وَكَأَيِّن). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic to make words flow better. Here it connects to قَرْيَةٍ (town/city) to show 'from a town'.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic word structure

steps.m: a village/town (a place where people gather and settle)
steps.n: The ة (taa marbutah) creates a feminine noun meaning 'place where people gather'
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is being acted upon by the word مِّن (from) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb. The و changes to ا at the end.
Added أ at the beginning to show causing someone else to wait. The sukun (ْ) helps pronunciation.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action. The ى changes to يْ before the تُ.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added ها which refers back to the feminine noun قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a basic feminine pronoun used to refer back to قَرْيَةٍ (town) which was mentioned before. In Arabic, 'town' is treated as feminine.

steps.m: and she/it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ - the town)
steps.n: The prefix و adds 'and' to connect this pronoun to what came before it. It's connecting this clause to the previous statement about the town.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Changes verb into a word describing someone who does the action. This describes the state of وَهِىَ (it/she) in the ayah.
Adds ة to match with وَهِىَ which is feminine
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing the state of the town (قَرْيَةٍ) at the time Allah gave it respite
Basic form of the word that shows one thing happens after another

Added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda (ّ) with fatha (َ) on last letter to show proper pronunciation. This word connects how Allah first gave them time (أَمْلَيْتُ) and then seized them (أَخَذْتُهَا).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The تُ suffix indicates first person singular past tense (I)
Added هَا at the end to show what was taken (referring back to قَرْيَةٍ 'town/city' mentioned earlier in the verse)
Basic preposition showing direction
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added ىَّ (pronounced 'ayya') at the end to show this means 'to me' specifically

steps.m: to move or change from one state to another

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make a basic noun meaning 'the act of changing'
Added مَ at the beginning to show where the action ends up
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the phrase 'and to Me is the destination'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on last letter, 3) Removing the middle weak letter و because it comes between two consonants in this form
The damma shows this word is in the subject position
Added the vocative particle يٰٓ to make it a call for attention
Added ها to make it address someone specifically

steps.m: to be social, to keep company with others

steps.n: The root letters أنس become نَاس (people) by dropping the first letter and adding a long 'aa' sound
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) adds emphasis to the word
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific people, not just any people
The ُ shows these are the people being spoken to in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ (O people!)
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding مَا changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction, like saying 'nothing but' or 'only'
The alif is lengthened at the end for emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
This is the basic personal pronoun meaning 'I' or 'me'. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added fatha marks to show the correct pronunciation: 'ana'
Added a special mark (۠) that shows this is being used as a separate pronoun. In this ayah, it connects to نَذِيرٌ (warner) to mean 'I am a warner'.
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this describes someone who does the warning. In this ayah, it's describing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ as a warner to people.
Added double-damma at the end to show this is describing someone in a general way, like saying "a warner" instead of "the warner"
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' and changed inside vowels to show it's describing something that makes things clear
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added tanween to show this describes the warner (نَذِيرٌ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added ال to make it specific. This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'the one who' in English.
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people (plural masculine)
Added ف at the beginning to show this comes as a result or sequence. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) that follows it.
Added vowel marks and changed spelling slightly to match Quranic writing style. The hamza on the alif shows it's at the beginning of a word part.
Adding alif makes it about having faith in something/someone

steps.m: they believed (subject of لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ - 'for them is forgiveness')
steps.n: Added وا۟ at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action. This word is connected to لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ in the ayah, showing that these believers will receive forgiveness.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense. This word is connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in this ayah.
Added وا to show a group of people did the action together. This matches with ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in describing what the believers did.
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one (believing). This shows they both believed AND did good deeds.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who has this quality
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple good deeds rather than people
Added the definite article and doubled the ص as a result
Changed to proper Quranic spelling with kasra ending showing it's the object of 'they did' (عَمِلُوا)
This is a basic word that shows something is being given or directed to someone. Here it connects to the word مَّغْفِرَةٌ (forgiveness) to show who will receive the forgiveness.

steps.m: for them (referring to those who believe and do good deeds)
steps.n: Added هُم which shows this is about a group of people (specifically referring back to 'those who believe and do good deeds' mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: to cover up or hide faults

steps.n: Adding مَ at the start with the vowel pattern changes this from an action to a thing related to that action
Adding ة changes it from a general concept of covering faults to a specific act of forgiveness
Adding shadda on م makes the forgiveness more emphasized/intense
Changes from action to the thing itself (like 'provide' to 'provision')
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this word to the previous word مَّغْفِرَةٌ (forgiveness)
Adds double dammah to make it indefinite (like saying 'a provision' instead of 'the provision')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The ي in the middle with kasra before it changes it from an action to a description
The double damma at the end shows this is describing something general (like saying 'a' instead of 'the'). In this ayah, it's describing the provision (رِزْقٌ) that Allah gives.
This is the basic form that points to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Added و at the beginning to connect it with what came before, like saying 'and'. Also added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it.

steps.m: to strive or move quickly towards a goal

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past
The ى at the end is changed to alif because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb
Added وْا۟ to show many people did this action. This word describes what the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ) did.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. It has no root since it's one of the basic building blocks of Arabic.
Added the kasra mark (ِ) under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fi'
Changed the ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to ءَايٰتِنَا (our signs) to show what something is in.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: The root letters were joined together and the first letter became a hamza with fatha. The middle letter 'ya' changed to a long 'aa' sound (alif) to make the word for 'sign' or 'miracle'.
Added ة to make it a singular noun referring to one specific sign or miracle
Changed to plural form by adding ات
Added kasra at end to show it's being possessed or after a preposition
Added نَا to show these are our signs

steps.m: to be unable to do something

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes it about competing or trying to outdo someone else
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'someone who' and changed vowels to show it's a person doing the competing/challenging

steps.m: those who try to frustrate/challenge (object of سَعَوْا)
steps.n: Added ين to show multiple people. This describes those who strive against Allah's signs in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to something far away. It's a complete word not derived from a root.
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show proper pronunciation
Added alif with maddah (ٰ) and small alif (ٓ) to show long vowel sound
Added hamza with kasra (ئِ) as a connecting sound

steps.m: those (pointing to something far away) (subject of أَصْحٰبُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun. In this ayah, this word is the subject of أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people), meaning 'those are the companions/people'.

steps.m: to be a close friend or companion

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changed from 'he accompanied' to 'companions' by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowels. Like saying 'friend' became 'friends'. Connected to الجَحِيمِ to mean 'companions of Hell'
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation with no meaning change
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a place or thing with intense burning

steps.m: the hellfire (object of أَصْحٰبُ - companions/people of)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end to show this is the object being possessed by أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people of)
Base negative particle ما that negates the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the Quran
Extended alif (ا → آ) for emphasis in recitation, doesn't change meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: he made (someone/something) go as a messenger
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start and sukun on middle letter shows causing someone/something to do the action

steps.m: We sent (as the subject of وَمَآ)
steps.n: Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action. This word follows وَمَآ (and not) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts from. Here it connects with قَبْلِكَ (before you) to show the starting point in time.
Root letters got vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the word mean 'before'. Comes after مِن in the ayah.

steps.m: before you (possessed by you, following مِن)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك (you) and kasra to show possession. The kasra is needed because the word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin. Here it combines with قَبْلِكَ to mean 'before you', describing the time before the Prophet.

steps.m: messenger (one who delivers messages as their role)
steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'one who does the sending'
Adds tanween kasra to show this is indefinite and in genitive case
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'news' instead of 'to tell news'
Adding يّ changes it from 'news' to 'related to news/prophecy'
The kasra (ِ) sound shows this is a permanent quality or role
Added two dots under (kasra) and a tail (tanween) because it's receiving the action from the word 'from/of' before it
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This is a basic exception particle.
Added long alif at the end to connect this word with what follows. In this ayah, it connects to إِذَا (when) to mean 'except when'.
Basic time particle that points to a specific moment
Adding ا makes it conditional - shows something happens whenever something else happens. Here it connects to تَمَنَّىٰٓ (he desired/recited) to show 'when he recited'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the wishing/desiring more intense or emphatic
Adding تَ shows the person is doing the action to/for themselves
The ى at the end shows this is a past tense verb that ends with a weak letter
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and putting sukun on ل makes the action more direct and forceful. The word الشَّيْطٰنُ (Shaytan) is doing this action of throwing/casting.
Added ي to turn the root meaning 'rebellious' into a noun meaning 'one who rebels'
Added ا to emphasize the meaning of being extremely rebellious
Makes it refer to the specific being known as Satan rather than any rebellious being

steps.m: the Satan (as the one doing the action of casting/throwing)
steps.n: Adds ُ to show Satan is the one performing the action of يُلْقِى (casting/throwing) in this verse
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else
The elongated ى (with ٓ) shows this word connects to the word after it (أُمْنِيَّتِهِۦ meaning 'his desire/recitation')
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (to wish) into a noun meaning the result of wishing/desiring
Changes to passive voice - something that was wished/desired
Adds ة at the end to make it mean 'a single wish' instead of wishes in general
Adds 'his/her' possession marker to show someone owns/has this wish
Adds masculine singular possessive pronoun suffix ه with prolongation ۦ. This word appears after في (in/into) in the ayah, showing what the devil throws into.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changes to present tense and shows this word is acting on what الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) casts

steps.m: and then He (اللَّهُ) removes (what الشَّيْطٰنُ casts)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens after Satan casts something. The subject here is Allah (اللَّهُ) who removes what Satan tries to cast.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Basic particle that connects to يُلْقِى (throws/casts) to show what is being thrown/cast
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and changing vowel pattern. This shows Satan is the one doing the throwing.
The ى at the end shows this is a complete present tense verb

steps.m: to rebel, to act defiantly, to be distant
one who rebels or acts defiantly
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) after first letter and alif (ا) after second letter to create a noun indicating a characteristic or quality. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb ألقى (cast/threw), so it takes the nominative case.
Added ي (yaa) after first letter to make the word stronger - like changing 'rebel' to 'super rebel'
Added ُ to show this is the doer of the action 'threw' in the verse
Added definite article to specify 'the' devil
Doubling the ش with shadda for emphasis, common in this word
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects what Allah does after erasing (يَنسَخُ) - first He erases, then He makes firm His verses.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show present tense form - sukun on first letter, kasra on middle letter, and damma at end

steps.m: he makes firm, he perfects (الله is the doer)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense. In this ayah, الله is the one doing this action of making firm.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who abolishes what Satan throws in)
steps.n: Added ال (the) with special spelling, plus a strong L sound (shadda), to transform the general word for 'god' into the special name of the One True God - Allah
Adding ة makes this a feminine noun meaning 'a sign'
Added ات to make it plural (more than one sign)
Added kasra at end to show it belongs to/connects with another word
Added هِ to show the signs belong to Him (Allah)
Added ۦ after هِ to show proper pronunciation of 'his'
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a unique proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of يَنسَخُ - erases)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action of erasing in this verse

steps.m: And Allah (as the doer of يَنسَخُ - erases)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement

steps.m: And Allah (as the doer of يَنسَخُ - erases)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a wasl form since it's not pronounced separately after the و
Base form I verb with characteristic voweling for verbs of knowing/understanding
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality
Added two dots (tanween) to show this is one of Allah's names/qualities, meaning He is the one who knows everything
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from an action (judging) to a special name showing that Allah has perfect wisdom as His forever quality
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future, and changed middle vowel to sukun
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose - this explains why something else happens
The ل gets a shadda because it comes after the word إِنَّ
This is a particle that in this context means 'whatever' and refers to what the Shaytan throws. It's the object (thing being acted upon) of the verb يَجْعَلَ (he makes/places).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense, with sukun and kasra vowels. The word الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) is doing this action.

steps.m: to be far away or rebel against authority

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added ي and ا to show someone who does this action intensely

steps.m: the one who rebels greatly (the devil)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific, and the ل assimilates into ش with a shadda

steps.m: the devil (as the doer of يُلْقِى - what he throws)
steps.n: Added U ending to show it's doing the action of throwing (يُلْقِى) in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he tested) to a noun (a test) by changing the vowels and adding ة at the end

steps.m: a trial/test (object of لِّيَجْعَلَ - that He may make)
steps.n: Added tanween (double fatha) to show this is the object being made into something in the phrase 'that He may make it a trial'
This is a plural relative pronoun meaning 'those who'. The ال is an integral part of the word, not a prefix. The shadda on ل shows emphasis.
Added لِ (meaning 'for/to') at the beginning of the word. The rest stays the same.

steps.m: for those who (connected to فِتْنَةً - a trial)
steps.n: Added emphasis to the ل with a shadda. This word connects to فِتْنَةً (trial/temptation) in the ayah, showing who the trial is for.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows the location where something is - inside their hearts.
Added kasra (ِ) under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'
Changed the regular ي to ى (alif maqsurah) at the end, which is how this word is written in the Quran
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'heart'
Changed the vowel pattern and added و to make it plural
Added هِم at the end to show possession - 'their hearts'
Root letters connected with vowels to make a noun meaning 'sickness' instead of the action 'to be sick'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it 'a sickness' instead of 'the sickness'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from verb 'became hard' to adjective meaning 'hard'
The doubled yaa makes it a stronger adjective
Added feminine marker to match with قلوب (hearts) which is feminine in Arabic
Added the definite article to specify these particular hard hearts
Added 'and' at beginning and kasra at end because it's connected to what comes before it in the verse
Forms the singular noun for 'heart' by adding vowel marks
Changes the word to broken plural form by changing internal vowels and adding و
Adds suffix showing possession by a group
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: and indeed/and certainly (connects to previous statement about those with diseased hearts)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to the previous one. This shows that this statement about wrongdoers is connected to what was just said about those with diseased hearts.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than two wrongdoers)
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and shadda on ظ because it comes after ال. Added fatha at end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or state
Added emphatic لَ at beginning to show certainty. This emphasizes that the wrongdoers are definitely in a state of opposition.

steps.m: to split or separate into opposing sides

steps.n: The ا makes it about two opposing sides splitting from each other
Added tanween kasra because this word comes after في (in) in the phrase 'لَفِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍ' meaning 'in far/deep opposition'
Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality. This word describes how deep or severe the division (شِقَاقٍۭ) is.

steps.m: far (describing شِقَاقٍۭ/division, showing it follows فِي)
steps.n: Added the kasratain (ٍ) at the end because this word comes after the word فِي (in/within) in the phrase فِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (in far division)
Adding يَ at the start shows this is happening now or in the future. The َ (fatha) at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood because it follows لِ (so that)

steps.m: so that he knows (purpose of previous action)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is expressing purpose ('in order to'). This connects to the previous actions in the verse to show their purpose

steps.m: and so that he knows (connected to previous actions)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statements in the verse, showing this is another purpose alongside the others
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (referring to those given knowledge in أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda to make it specific and point to particular people - in this case, those who were given knowledge
Changed to passive voice by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and kasra (ِ) on middle letter. The ي becomes َ at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added و after first letter because أ and ي are weak letters that change in this pattern
Added وا at the end to show multiple people received the action. The final weak letter ي is dropped. This word is what the people knew in the phrase 'so that those who were given knowledge may know'.
Changes the action 'to know' into the thing 'knowledge'. This word is what was 'given' in the phrase 'those who were given knowledge'

steps.m: the knowledge (object of أُوتُوا - were given)
steps.n: The word 'knowledge' got 'the' (ال) added at the start and a mark (َ) at the end to show it's receiving the action of being given
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain and definite

steps.m: that it (referring to الْحَقُّ - the truth)
steps.n: Adds هُ which refers back to الْحَقُّ (the truth) that comes right after this word in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled letter shows emphasis and turns the verb into a noun
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific truth rather than truth in general
The ُ ending shows this is the subject of أَنَّهُ (that it is) in this ayah

steps.m: from (the truth that comes from رَّبِّكَ)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing source or origin. In this ayah, it connects the truth (الْحَقُّ) to its source - your Lord (رَّبِّكَ).

steps.m: to nurture and care for completely

steps.n: Doubling the ب shows complete/intense nurturing
Added shadda and kasra to show this is 'my lord' or 'the lord of' something
Added كَ to show this is your Lord. This word comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, believe in

steps.n: Connected the root letters together to form the basic verb
Added present tense prefix and vowel pattern to show ongoing belief/trust
Added plural ending to show multiple people believing

steps.m: and so they believe (in response to ٱلْحَقُّ - the truth)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this belief is a result of recognizing the truth mentioned earlier in the verse
Added وا to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Basic preposition showing how something is done or connected

steps.m: in it/him (referring to الْحَقُّ - the truth)
steps.n: Adds pronoun 'it/him' referring back to 'the truth' mentioned earlier in the verse. The ۦ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with feminine/second person prefix

steps.m: so that she/you may become humble (connected to يَعْلَمَ - may know)
steps.n: Added فَ to show sequence and changed final vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood after 'so that'
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb فَتُخْبِتَ (so they may humble themselves) to show what something is for.
Adds the pronoun 'him/it' to show who the action is for. The ۥ mark after هُ is just to show proper pronunciation.
The root meaning of turning/transforming becomes a noun for heart, since the heart is seen as something that turns or changes (both physically and emotionally)
Changes the word to refer to multiple hearts by adding و between letters and changing vowels to ُ. This form is used for groups of things that are related.
Adds هُمْ at the end to show these hearts belong to them. The ُ before هُمْ connects it to the word.
Adding the basic connecting word 'and'
Adding إِ (indeed) to make it more emphatic
Adding نَّ completes the emphasis particle. This word connects to اللَّهَ in the next part to mean 'and indeed Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers specifically to The One True God.
The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end to show it's receiving the action of guidance. Think of it like Allah being the receiver, not the doer, in this sentence.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from verb 'guided' to noun 'a guide' by adding alif and tanwin kasra
Adds لَ at beginning to add emphasis, meaning 'surely/indeed'. This word is describing how Allah guides those who believe.
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال with shadda to make it specific and definite
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of ءَامَنُوٓا۟ (they believed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made someone feel secure/he believed
steps.n: The extra alif at start changes meaning to 'causing/making' someone do the action
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word صِرٰطٍ (path) to show 'to a path'.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) above the final letter to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Adding the basic vowels to make the word readable
Changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation in Quranic text
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a path') and is being described by the next word مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (straight)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who seeks to be straight/upright
steps.n: Added مُسْتَ at the start to mean 'someone who wants to' or 'tries to' be straight - like adding 'seeker of' before a word
The و changes to ي because it's between two kasras (ِ sounds)
Added shadda on م and tanween kasra at the end because it's describing the word 'path' (صِرٰطٍ) in the ayah
This is the basic negative particle لا that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This وَلَا negates the verb يَزَالُ that comes after it, meaning 'they will not cease to'
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the 'aa' sound in the middle
Added يَ at start to show present tense and ُ at end to show it's doing the action. When used after وَلَا (not), it means 'continues' instead of 'stops'.
Added ي to make the basic relative pronoun
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added ين to make it plural. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to reject, to cover up, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word is part of the phrase 'those who disbelieved continue to be...'

steps.m: in (connects مِرْيَةٍ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects the word مِرْيَةٍ (doubt) to the previous phrase, showing they are in a state of doubt.

steps.m: in (connects مِرْيَةٍ to what comes before)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'one specific doubt'

steps.m: any doubt (in فِى doubt about it)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to فِى (in) from earlier in the verse, meaning 'in doubt'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Added sukun to show end of word.

steps.m: from him/it (referring to being in doubt about the Quran)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ to show who/what is being referred to
Added shadda on م to show emphasis in pronunciation. This is a common change when مِن appears at the start of a word.
This is the basic form of the word that means reaching an end point. It's a particle (tool word) that doesn't come from a root.
The shadda (double letter mark) makes the meaning stronger, showing a definite endpoint.

steps.m: until (connects to تَأْتِيَهُمُ - comes to them)
steps.n: The ىٰ at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'comes to them' (تَأْتِيَهُمُ).
Connected the root letters together

steps.n: Added ت at beginning to show present tense feminine form. This matches with الساعة (the Hour) which is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: she/it comes to them (الساعة comes to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being affected by the action (them). This refers back to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved) mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: a period of measured time, an hour
steps.n: Transforms the root into a feminine noun meaning a specific time/hour

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding 'the' (ال) makes it refer specifically to the Day of Judgment instead of just any hour

steps.m: the Hour (subject of تَأْتِيَهُمُ - comes to them)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of 'coming' in تَأْتِيَهُمُ

The word already has tanween - it describes how suddenly/unexpectedly something happens

steps.n: This is a basic conjunction that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects two things that could come to the disbelievers: either the Hour (ٱلسَّاعَةُ) or the punishment of a barren day (عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَقِيمٍ)
Connected the root letters together to form the basic verb
The ي at the start shows it's happening now and means 'he'
Added هم at the end to mean 'them' - like adding a receiver of the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun describing the result of the action
Adding damma shows this word owns or possesses the word that comes after it (يَوْمٍ/day), making it mean 'punishment of a day'
Root letters put into a simple noun pattern with fatha-sukoon to make the word for 'day'
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is possessed by another word. This word belongs to عَذَابُ (punishment), making it 'punishment of a day'.
Added vowels and ي to make an intense adjective. This word describes يَوْمٍ (day) in the ayah, making it mean 'a devastating day'

steps.m: devastating (describes يَوْمٍ 'day', showing it follows another word)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word follows يَوْمٍ (day) in the phrase 'a devastating day'
Changes the concept of ruling/owning into the abstract noun for the power or authority itself
Adds ال to show this is referring to THE authority/dominion (not just any authority)
The ُ shows this word is the subject doing the action in يَحْكُمُ (judges/rules) in this ayah
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something

steps.m: on that day (when the Kingdom belongs to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding ئِذٍ changes 'day' to mean 'on that specific day' - referring to the Day of Judgment when Allah has complete authority
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on the ل showing emphasis
Added ل at the beginning which means 'for' or 'to', and the ا from الله is dropped when combined
Added kasra (ِ) under the first ل to help with pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or ownership
Added shadda on first ل for emphasis, showing this is a strong statement about something belonging to Allah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end. This word connects to بَيْنَهُمْ showing who he judges between.
Adding vowels to make the word complete and readable
Adds attached pronoun suffix هُمْ meaning 'them' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers to those being judged on the Day of Judgment.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one

steps.m: and so those who (connecting to يَحْكُمُ - He judges)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this follows from the previous statement about Allah judging between them
Adding vowels to show this is a feeling/emotion verb
Adding alif after hamza changes the meaning to actively choosing to trust/believe rather than just feeling safe
Added وا to show many people did this action. This word describes الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding وا makes it refer to a group of males who did the action
وَ connects this to ءَامَنُوا to show these are two actions done by the same group
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
This form describes someone or something that has the quality of being good/righteous
Adds the feminine plural ending to make it refer to multiple righteous things/deeds
The ٱلْ makes it specific and the ِ shows it's being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا۟ (they did/performed)
This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows that the believers will be in the gardens of paradise (جَنَّاتِ).
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) between the root letters to make a word meaning 'something that covers'

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered with plants)
steps.n: The doubled ن (shadda) shows completeness of covering, while ة makes it a specific instance
Changes single garden to multiple gardens by adding ات
The ِ ending shows this word follows في (in/at/on) and is connected to النَّعِيمِ (pleasure/delight)

steps.m: to be blessed, comfortable, or at ease

steps.n: Changes the action into a noun describing the state or feeling itself
Shows this word describes what kind of gardens they are - gardens OF bliss
Adds 'the' at the start and doubles the ن because of how the sounds combine
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects clauses
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adds 'and' to connect this with what came before. This word is the subject of كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth, to cover up or hide something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. This word describes what 'those who' (وَٱلَّذِينَ) did.
Adding وا changes it to mean 'they' (masculine plural) instead of 'he'. This matches with وَٱلَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows they strongly or repeatedly denied something

steps.m: they denied intensely (subject of عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ)
steps.n: Changed to show multiple people did the action. This group will receive the humiliating punishment mentioned after.
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'sign'. The first two letters combine into a long آ sound.
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs or miracles
Adds 'our' to show these are Allah's signs being mentioned
Adds بِ at the beginning to show these signs were the means by which they denied the truth. This word connects to كَذَّبُوا (they denied) in the ayah.
This is a demonstrative pronoun for pointing to things far away
The word 'those' got a ك (ka) added at the end to point to something far away, like saying 'those over there'

steps.m: so those (referring to those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what was mentioned before (their disbelief)
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) to show who will receive it.
Adds هُمْ to show this refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier (وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟) who will receive the punishment
Added alif after second letter to make it into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added double dammah to make it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'one who humiliates/disgraces others'
Adding shadda on the meem makes the meaning stronger
Added tanween to show this describes the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things
Added ال at the beginning and ي at the end to make it point to someone or something specific
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people or things
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before, and added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif changes it from just 'leaving' to specifically 'migrating'

steps.m: they migrated (subject of قُتِلُوا - were killed)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word describes those who migrated and were then killed in Allah's cause.
Basic preposition showing location or path. Here it connects 'migrated' (هَاجَرُوا) with 'path' (سَبِيلِ) to show where they migrated.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Quran's writing style. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Root letters connected with fatHa vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'path'
Adding ي and changing vowels creates the common noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'path of Allah'
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it specifically refer to Allah, the One True God
Added shadda on ل and kasra at the end to show ownership/possession - like saying 'Allah's' in English

steps.m: then (connects this part to what came before)
steps.n: Basic form of the word that shows sequence of events. In this ayah, it connects what happens to those who migrate in Allah's path with what comes next.

steps.m: then (connects this part to what came before)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda (ّ) with fatha (َ) on last letter to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show multiple people were affected
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects 'were killed' with 'died', showing both groups will receive provision from Allah.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters spell 'he died' in past tense
Changed مَوَتَ to مَاتَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لَ to show emphasis and certainty
Added نَّ for extra emphasis and changed final vowel to fatha

steps.m: most surely He will provide them
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who receives the provision
Changed the root into the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of لَيَرْزُقَنَّهُمُ - will provide them)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is the one doing the providing in this verse. Changed alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to provide, sustain, or give provisions

steps.n: Changed from action (providing) to the thing itself (provision) by adding vowels to make a noun
Added tanween to show this is the object being provided. This word is what Allah will provide to those who migrated and died in His cause.
