Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the sky' instead of just 'sky'. The س gets doubled when ال is added.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being held up (by Allah in يُمْسِكُ - He holds), and lengthened the alif
This particle connects the previous verb يُمْسِكُ (He holds) with what is being held - that the sky would fall (تَقَعَ). It introduces what is being prevented.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
The word changes from past tense to present tense by adding ت at the start. When أَن comes before it, it means 'that it falls'
This is a preposition that shows something is positioned above or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show what might fall upon it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun
Adds kasra to show this word comes after the preposition فِى (in)
Makes the noun definite by adding ال. This word comes after فِى (in) in the phrase 'what is in the earth'
This is a basic exception word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before it.
Adding the shadda and alif makes the exception stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's used to show that something can only happen with Allah's permission.
Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra at start and sukun in middle
Added 'hi' (ـهِ) at the end to mean 'His', showing it belongs to Allah

steps.m: by His permission (showing how something happens with Allah's approval)
steps.n: Added 'by' at the beginning to show how something happens. The ۦٓ at the end is just for pronunciation.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
steps.n: Adding the kasra and shadda makes this word stronger and shows it affects the next word (اللَّهَ). It's like adding an exclamation mark in English to show importance.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers specifically to the One True God in Islam.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ - Kind, Merciful)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is being described. Also added the shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
steps.n: The root becomes the collective noun 'people'
Adding alif creates the pattern for a group/collection of people
Adds 'the' to make it specific people rather than just any people
Adds 'with' or 'towards' at the beginning and 'i' at the end to show relationship
This is the basic meaning of being kind and gentle to others
Added و after second letter and vowels to make it mean someone who is very kind
Added tanween to show this is describing Allah in this ayah

steps.m: truly very kind and gentle (describing اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to emphasize that Allah is definitely very kind
This root carries the basic idea of showing kindness and care to others
Adding the vowels and ي creates an intensive form showing someone who has a lot of this quality
The double mark (tanween) at the end shows this is an adjective describing Allah
The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after لَرَءُوفٌ - this is just a pronunciation rule
This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah in this context, since the next part of the ayah describes His actions (giving life and causing death)

steps.m: and He (who gives life and death)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This connects to the next part which explains what He does (gives life, causes death, then gives life again).
This is the base word meaning 'possessor' or 'owner of' something
Adding ال and shadda changes this into a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence. Here it connects to the verb أَحْيَاكُمْ (gave you life)
Changed to standard Uthmani script with wavy alif and extended ya. This is just a writing style change that doesn't affect meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes the meaning from 'to live' to 'to make something live/give life to'. Like changing 'live' to 'bring to life'.

steps.m: he gave life to you all (subject of هُوَ in وَهُوَ)
steps.n: The suffix كم makes this verb take 'you all' as its object
Basic form of the word that shows sequence - like saying 'and then' or 'after that' in English
Added vowel marks and emphasis (shadda) to show proper pronunciation. This word connects 'causes you to die' with 'gives you life' to show these happen in sequence.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense and changed internal vowels to show causative meaning (making something happen)

steps.m: he causes you all to die
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is happening to 'you all'
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then, next, afterward (shows sequence of events)
steps.n: Adding the damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) with fatha (َ) makes the word stronger and clearer in showing that one thing definitely follows another
Added sukun on first letter and kasra on second letter to prepare for the causative meaning
The prefix يُ shows this is happening now or in the future. This form is used because Allah continuously gives life.

steps.m: He gives life to you all (subject of هُوَ)
steps.n: The suffix كُمْ means 'you all' and shows who receives the action. This word connects back to هُوَ (He) at the start of the verse, showing that Allah is the one who gives life.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that mankind is ungrateful. It makes the next word (ٱلْإِنسٰنَ) take a special ending.

steps.m: to be close and friendly with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'friendly/social' became a noun for all humans, since humans are social beings
Adding ان makes it mean 'one who has the quality of being social/human'
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end to show this human is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to reject or hide truth, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who does this action intensely
Added لَ to emphasize that they are truly/definitely ungrateful
Added tanween to show this describes people in general, not a specific person
The doubled ل (shadda) shows completeness, while the damma makes it into a noun
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' something (in this case, for every nation/أُمَّةٍ)
Two changes: 1) Added shadda to لِ for emphasis, 2) Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the word 'nation' that comes after it

steps.m: to be in front or lead others

steps.n: Adding damma changes it from a verb root to a noun meaning a group or community
Adding the ة makes it a feminine noun, commonly used for nations/communities

steps.m: a community (possessed by لِّكُلِّ - for every)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this community belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, it belongs to 'every/each' as shown by لِّكُلِّ before it)

steps.m: to make something into something else, to set, to place

steps.n: Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense verb form. The final fatha will change in the next step.

steps.m: we made/set (connected to مَنسَكًا - a ritual/rite)
steps.n: Changed the final fatha to sukun (ْ) before adding نَا which shows 'we' did the action. This word connects to مَنسَكًا which explains what was made/set.
Added مَ at the beginning to show where the action happens. This word is what was 'made' or 'established' (object of جَعَلْنَا - we made/established)

steps.m: a place of ritual (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what was established/made and to make it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
This is a standalone pronoun that refers to multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it's connected to نَاسِكُو (performers) to show who is doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action
Made the word plural to show multiple people performing rituals
Added 'him' to show who the ritual is being performed for
Added هُ to show what is being performed. This word connects back to مَنسَكًا (ritual) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: so not (connects to يُنٰزِعُنَّكَ - they dispute with you)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence. Here it connects to the next word يُنٰزِعُنَّكَ (they dispute with you), showing 'so they should not dispute with you'

steps.m: to dispute or pull away from

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added alif to show mutual action between two parties
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added emphasis particle نّ to show certainty

steps.m: they should surely dispute with you
steps.n: Added كَ (ka) at the end to show they are disputing with 'you', and the alif is written as a small alif above (ٰ) but pronounced the same way
Basic preposition showing location or context. Here it connects to الْأَمْرِ (the matter) to show 'in the matter'
Added vowel marks to change from action to noun - now it means the thing itself rather than doing it
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in) in the ayah
Added sukun on first letter and damma on second letter to make it a command form
Added ٱ at start and dropped final و because it's a weak letter at the end of a command

steps.m: and call out! (connecting to فَلَا يُنٰزِعُنَّكَ - so do not let them dispute with you)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous statement
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this connects to the word after it (رَبِّكَ - your Lord). This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to care for, raise, develop, sustain
Lord (the one who nurtures and sustains)
steps.n: Connected the root letters and added shadda to show intensity, making it mean 'the one who completely nurtures'
Added kasra to show this word connects to something after it

steps.m: your Lord (the one calling to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show it means 'your Lord'. This word is part of the phrase 'call to your Lord' (وَٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ)
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain
Added كَ at the end to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, Allah is speaking directly to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
Basic preposition meaning 'upon'. The ى with small alif (ىٰ) is just a spelling variation of regular ى.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely or surely true.
Added tanween (double fatha) and changed final ي to ى to show this is a general concept. In this ayah, it refers specifically to the straight path mentioned right after it (مُّسْتَقِيمٍ).
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'that which is straight'

steps.m: a straight path (description of هُدًى - guidance)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and tanween at end to show this describes the guidance mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: if/whether (introduces condition about جٰدَلُوكَ - they argue with you)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible scenario - in this case, about people arguing or disputing

steps.m: and if (connects condition about arguing to previous statement)
steps.n: The و (and) connects this condition about people arguing to what came before it. The full phrase وَإِن جٰدَلُوكَ means 'and if they argue with you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after ج to show two people arguing with each other
Adding و shows a group of people did this
Adding كَ shows who they were arguing with - you
Changed to a command by adding a 'u' sound (ُ) and making it shorter - like telling someone 'Say!'

steps.m: then say! (responding to جٰدَلُوكَ - if they argue with you)
steps.n: Added 'fa' (then/so) at the start to show this is what to do after they argue, and a connecting vowel at the end to join with the next word
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to make it mean 'most' of this quality. This form is used to show Allah has the highest degree of this attribute.
This word connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you do) by referring to the actions being done
The بِ shows how Allah knows - through or by means of your actions
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you (all) work/act/do (in response to what they argue/dispute)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to a group. This verb relates to the earlier جٰدَلُوكَ (they argue with you) in the ayah, showing what the group does in response to the arguing.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
The ي at the beginning shows it's happening now, and the ُ marks show it's active voice
Added vowel marks to make it a preposition. The fatha at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it.

steps.m: between you all (in يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ - He judges between you all)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word is part of the phrase 'He judges between you all' in this ayah.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of judging from the verb يَحْكُمُ in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'stood up' into noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added ة to make it refer to the specific event of resurrection
Added ٱلْ to specify this is THE resurrection (Day of Judgment)

steps.m: the resurrection (in relation to يَوْمَ - Day of)
steps.n: Changed to 'of the resurrection' by adding kasra ending, since it comes after 'Day of'
Added kasra under ف to show it's a preposition meaning 'in'
Added ما which means 'what' to make the combined meaning 'in what'. This word connects to تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ) in this ayah to mean 'in what you differ'.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The word changes from 'being' to a command 'be!' by dropping the و and adding a sukun
Added تُ at the end to change from a command (be!) to 'I was' in past tense
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to make it plural 'you'
Adding the kasra vowel mark to show it's a preposition meaning 'in'

steps.m: in it (referring to what تَخْتَلِفُونَ you differ/disagree)
steps.n: Adding هِ at the end shows what the preposition 'in' refers to. Here it refers back to the things being disagreed about, mentioned in تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ/disagree).
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added letters to show people disagreeing with each other (back and forth)
Changed from past tense 'he disagreed' to present tense 'you/she disagree' by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people are involved in the disagreement
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs after it to negative past tense
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'. In this ayah, it's asking 'Don't you know?' expecting the answer 'Yes, I do know.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ت at start changes it from 'he knew' to 'you know'
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of sentences, like saying 'that' in English. Here it connects 'Do you not know' with 'Allah knows'.
Adding the shadda (double letter mark) makes the meaning stronger, showing certainty. It emphasizes that Allah definitely knows what's in the heavens and earth.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he knows (what is in السَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: The يَ at the start shows it's happening now, the ُ at the end shows 'he' is doing it, and the ْ (sukun) helps with pronunciation
This word asks about or refers to things. Here it's being used to say 'what' Allah knows, connecting to the verb يَعْلَمُ (He knows) in this ayah.
This shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heaven' (السَّمَآءِ) to show where Allah knows what happens.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high/elevated
Adds 'the' (ال) to the word, making it specific which sky/heaven we're talking about
Changes ending to show it comes after في (in), making it mean 'of the sky'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun
Added the definite article to specify we're talking about THE earth
Added kasra to show this word follows في (in/on) in the ayah
Added و to connect this word with السماء (heaven/sky) mentioned before it
This is a basic particle used to connect ideas or show conditions
Adding the shadda changes this from a simple connector to a strong emphasis particle. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something (ذٰلِكَ - 'that') is truly easy for Allah.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are close by
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show extended distance
Adding لِ changes it to point to something far away instead of nearby

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to what's written in كِتٰبٍ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it more specific about what we're pointing to. In this ayah, it's pointing to what's written in the book (كِتٰبٍ)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heaven' (السَّمَآءِ), showing that Allah knows what is in the heaven.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changes from verb 'to write' into noun 'book/written record'
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style, but the meaning stays the same
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (like 'a book') and follows the word فِى (in) in the ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to help with pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) to make the emphasis stronger. This word introduces and emphasizes 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) in the phrase 'indeed that is in a book'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's elongated when reading
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to what Allah knows)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word and makes it point to something specific. Here it refers back to what Allah knows in the heavens and earth mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is positioned above or resting on top of something else. In this ayah, it connects to Allah's name to show that something is easy for Him.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic.
Added the kasra at the end to show possession, since this word belongs to كِتٰبٍ (book) in the ayah. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
The root letters convey the basic meaning of ease and simplicity
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective by adding ي between س and ر, changing the middle vowel to kasra, and adding tanween damma at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows the action is happening now
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action
Adding و with fatha at the beginning connects this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it. In this ayah, it connects with the word دُونِ to mean 'besides/other than'.
Adding damma (ُ) changes it to a noun meaning 'below/beneath'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing it means 'besides/other than Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

Added ال to make it definite and specific, doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it. This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to what is being worshipped. It connects to the verb يَعْبُدُونَ (they worship) in this ayah.
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Basic negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يُنَزِّلْ) to show it didn't happen in the past. Think of it like adding 'did not' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done multiple times or with intensity
Adding يُ at the beginning and changing vowels makes it present tense

steps.m: he sends down repeatedly (negated by لَمْ)
steps.n: Adding sukun at the end because it follows لَمْ (not) in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or what it's connected to. Here it connects knowledge (عِلْمٌ) with what came before.
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what the preposition connects to. This refers back to what was mentioned before in the ayah.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
The root conveys the basic concept of authority or power
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'power' by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon)
Added ان to make it into the common noun meaning authority or power

steps.m: any authority (object of يُنَزِّلْ - send down)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it refers to what Allah did not send down as authority for their worship.
Basic particle that can mean 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah, it's used for negation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement. Here it connects to لَيْسَ (is not) to emphasize the negation.
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
Adding the basic vowels to make it a complete word meaning 'is not'
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُم (hum) meaning 'them' to لَ (la) meaning 'for' to say 'for them'
This is a preposition that shows how something is done
Added هِ to show who/what is being referred to. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This word connects to سُلْطٰنًا (authority) in the ayah, showing they worship without any authority for it.
This is the basic meaning of knowing or understanding something
Changed the verb (to know) into a noun (knowledge) by adding the vowels ِ and ْ
Added ٌ (tanween damma) to show this word is the subject of لَيْسَ (is not) in this ayah
This is a special word that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used with لَيْسَ to emphasize the negation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This combination is used to add another negative statement after the first one.
This root relates to doing wrong or being unjust
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'someone who does this action'
Makes the word plural - means 'multiple people who do wrong'
Adds 'the' to make it specific wrongdoers
Adds 'for/to' at the beginning

steps.m: from/of (connects دُونِ to what comes after it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word دُونِ (besides/instead of) to what comes after it.
This root relates to helping or supporting
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed vowels and added ي to make it mean 'someone who helps' (like turning 'help' into 'helper')
Added two dots under to make it mean 'a helper' instead of 'the helper'

The shadda (ّ) was added to the ن, not a tanwin. The tanwin kasra (ٍ) was already there in both forms. The shadda shows this word connects to what came before it. It describes how the wrongdoers have no helper.

steps.m: when (introduces the condition of تُتْلَىٰ - when [verses] are recited)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces what happens when Allah's verses are recited.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition of تُتْلَىٰ - when [verses] are recited)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The و changes to ا at the end of past tense verbs.
Changed to present passive form by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. Shows something is being done to the subject rather than the subject doing it.
Changed يُ to تُ to show this is happening to something feminine (like the word آيات/verses mentioned in the ayah)
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show this is the full form of the word
This is a common preposition (على) combined with a pronoun (هم) meaning 'them'

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear
steps.n: The root transforms into a noun meaning 'signs' or 'verses'
Adds a vowel mark to help pronounce the word
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end
Adds damma to show this word is in the nominative case (subject)
Adds 'our' to show these are Allah's signs
Added نَا to show these are our signs. This word is the subject of تُتْلَىٰ (are recited).
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to create the basic adjective form meaning 'clear'
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple clear signs or evidences
Added marks at the end to show this word is receiving the action (like when you say 'I gave the signs' - the signs receive the giving)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changes past tense 'he recognized' to present tense 'you recognize'. This word is part of what 'they are about to' (يَكَادُونَ) do in the verse.
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. Here it shows the location where you can see/recognize something - in their faces.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed the word to show many faces by adding the و between letters and putting ُ marks on the first two letters
The ِ at the end shows these faces belong to 'those who disbelieved' in this ayah
Basic demonstrative word that points to something
Changed to relative pronoun form that connects descriptions to things or people
Added ال (the) and doubled the first letter to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Changed to plural form by adding ين at the end. This word describes the people who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added damma at the end to show this is the doer of the action
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action. This word is what Allah promised the fire to in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he rejected'
Added مُ at start to change from 'to reject' into 'something rejected'
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters. The و of the root changes to ا in this form.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and changed final vowel to ُ

steps.m: they almost do something (referring to the disbelievers who are about to attack those who recite Allah's verses)
steps.n: Added ون to show plural masculine subject. This word connects to the next verb يَسْطُونَ (they attack), showing what the disbelievers are about to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to make it present tense. The middle letter gets sukun and the last letter gets damma. The weak letter returns to و.

steps.m: they attack violently (referring to the disbelievers attacking those who recite Allah's verses)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to how the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) react aggressively towards those reciting Allah's verses.
This is the basic form of the word that connects different parts of a sentence, like saying 'who' or 'that' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding بِ at the beginning shows how something is done or what it's done with

steps.m: to follow, recite, or read (especially sacred texts)

steps.n: Connected the root letters ت-ل-و with short 'a' vowels (فتحة) to make 'he recited' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they recite (referring to those who disbelieve)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers to the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) in the ayah.
This is a common preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon them (the disbelievers who see our clear signs)
steps.n: The هِمْ at the end shows it's happening to multiple male people. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers who see Allah's clear signs being recited.
Forms the basic singular noun for 'sign' by combining the root letters with vowels. The middle ي becomes ا due to weak letter rules.
Forms regular feminine plural by adding ات suffix. This word is the subject of the passive verb تُتْلَى (is recited), so it takes the nominative case.

steps.m: our signs (object of تُتْلَىٰ - being recited)
steps.n: Adding نَا ('our') shows these signs belong to Allah. This word is what is being recited (the object, not subject, of تُتْلَىٰ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This makes it a direct command to one person.

steps.m: to inform or tell important news
steps.n: The word becomes a noun meaning the news or information itself
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Changed from 'he informed' to 'I inform' by adding أُ at start and changing vowels to show present tense
Added كُم to show who is being informed
Added فَ to show this follows from something before

steps.m: so shall I inform you all?
steps.n: Added أَ at start to make it a question
The root letters combine to form the basic noun meaning evil
The last two letters combine with a shadda (ّ) to intensify the meaning, making it stronger
Adding بِ shows this evil is what they attack with. This word connects to يَسْطُونَ (they attack) in the ayah.
Adding tanween (ٍ) shows this is indefinite - meaning any kind of evil, not a specific evil
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or something
Starting with the basic pointing word ذا and adding the alif above (ٰ) to show something far away
Added لِ to show we're pointing to something far away (like saying 'that over there')
Added كُمُ to show this is being pointed out to multiple people (you all)
The root meaning of illumination becomes a noun meaning 'light'
Root ن-و-ر becomes نَّار (fire) by changing و to ا and adding shadda to make it stronger
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific fire
The ُ shows this is the subject of وَعَدَهَا (He promised it)
Added fathas to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: he promised her (referring to النار/the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها to show who receives the promise. Here it refers to the Fire (النار) that Allah promises to those who disbelieve.
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific/definite - 'the' god instead of just any god
Added damma (ُ) to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Added ن and fatha to make it plural. This changes it from referring to one person/thing to many people/things.
Added ال at beginning with shadda on the following letter. This makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'.

steps.m: to deny or reject truth, to cover up what is right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb. This word is part of the group that يَكَادُونَ (they are about to) refers to.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. The small mark ۟ above the و is just to help with pronunciation.
Changes from basic root to past tense verb expressing strong disapproval

steps.m: and how evil! (connected to النَّارُ/the Fire)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this statement about how evil the Fire is with the previous statement

steps.m: to become, to arrive at a destination
steps.n: Basic root meaning showing the concept of reaching an end point or destination
Adding مَ at the beginning creates a noun for where something ends up
The ُ shows this word is the subject completing the sentence
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific destination rather than any destination

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: Adding damma to show it's in nominative case
Adds يا (ya) at the start to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!'
Adds ها to make it address people directly, like saying 'O you people!'
Changes from verb 'to be social' to noun meaning 'social beings/people'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: to strike, to give (as an example)
was struck/given (as an example to مَثَلٌ)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice pattern by adding damma and kasra vowel marks. In this ayah, it means 'an example was given' since it's followed by مَثَلٌ (example)

steps.m: to be like something or give an example

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates the basic noun. This word is what was 'struck' or 'put forward' (ضُرِبَ) in this ayah.
Adding tanween damma makes it 'an example' instead of 'the example'
This is the basic concept of hearing or listening
Adding the vowel marks changes it from a concept into a past tense verb meaning 'he heard'
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the action intensively
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is happening in the present tense
Added وا at the end to change from 'he listens' to 'all of you listen!' (makes it a command for a group)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for/to him/it (referring to ذُبَابًا - a fly)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه which refers back to 'a fly' (ذُبَابًا) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: for/to him/it (referring to ذُبَابًا - a fly)
steps.n: Added pronunciation helpers: ۥ to show the 'hu' sound and ٓ to show slight elongation
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word meaning 'if' to an emphasis word meaning 'indeed'. This word emphasizes the truth of what comes after it in the sentence.
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word is the subject of تَدْعُونَ (you call/invoke) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix for 'you'. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to ُ in present tense.
Added نَ to show multiple people are being addressed. This word is addressing the disbelievers who pray to false deities.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with the word دُونِ to mean 'from besides/other than'.

steps.m: to be below, beneath, or less than

steps.n: Adding damma changes it from a root to a usable word meaning 'below'
Added kasra to show connection to the following word Allah. This creates the meaning 'besides Allah' or 'other than Allah'
Added ال and merged it with the word to create the proper name Allah. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection. The alif at the start becomes a wasl form (ٱ).
A particle that makes something impossible in the future. Here it negates يَخْلُقُوا۟ (they create), showing they will never be able to create.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they create/are creating (referring to false deities who cannot create even a fly)
steps.n: Adds plural masculine ending to show multiple subjects. In this ayah, it refers to false deities who are being challenged that they cannot create even a fly.
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the insect that buzzes/flutters
Added -an ending to show it's the thing they cannot create (the object/target)
Basic conjunction particle that connects this phrase to what came before
Added لَو which is a conditional particle meaning 'if'
The kasra is added because of what comes after it (grammar rule), not to make it more emphatic. The meaning stays the same as 'and if'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added ا and ت to show people doing the action together or to themselves
Changed to show a group doing the action together
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل
Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who the preposition is directed to. This combination is the object of the command 'listen' in the ayah.
Added the small و (ۥ) which is a pronunciation helper showing that the هُ is pronounced with a long 'u' sound
Combines و (and) with إن (if) to form a conditional phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ and changing vowels

steps.m: he snatches away from them (referring to the flies mentioned in ذُبَابًا)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix هُمُ meaning 'them'. This refers back to the flies (ذُبَابًا) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to buzz, flutter, or fly around
steps.n: Root related to movement, from which 'fly' is derived
Changes from root meaning to word for group of flies
Added ال with hamzat wasl (ٱ) to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Root related to willing or wishing
Added vowels to make the basic noun form. The hamza at the end comes from the root letter أ, and the ي gets a sukoon (ْ) to show it's silent.
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is the object of the sentence
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
The ن from the previous word combines with the ل, shown by the shadda. This is a pronunciation change that doesn't affect meaning.
Basic meaning of rescuing
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added Form X pattern to show seeking or requesting the action
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding و
Added هُ to show what is being rescued
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the fly (ذُبَابًا) mentioned earlier.
Basic meaning of weakness
Adding vowels to show this is a verb about becoming weak
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'he sought' into 'seeker/one who seeks' by following the faa'il pattern
Adding ال causes the first letter to get a shadda

steps.m: the seeker (subject of ضَعُفَ - became weak)
steps.n: This word is the subject of the verb ضَعُفَ (became weak) in this ayah
Basic meaning of seeking
Changed from 'seeking' to 'being sought' - like changing from chasing to being chased
Adds ُ at the end to show this word is connected to 'the seeker' mentioned before - they are both doing the same action
Adds the definite article to specify a particular sought after thing
Adds 'and' (wa) at the start
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (قَدَرُوا۟) mean 'did not'. In this ayah, it negates how people valued or measured Allah's true worth.

steps.m: to measure or estimate correctly, to appreciate the true worth of something
he measured/estimated correctly (subject of negation ما)
steps.n: This is the basic past tense form. In this ayah, it's being negated by ما which means 'they did not...'

steps.m: they measured/estimated correctly (subject of negation ما)
steps.n: The suffix وا changes 'he' to 'they (masculine plural)'. In this ayah, this refers to those who don't appreciate Allah's true worth.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Added ال with special spelling (ٱ), doubled the ل with shadda, and added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence. This is the proper name for the one true God in Islam.

steps.m: to measure, estimate, determine true value

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (fatha) and doubled middle letter (shadda) to make a word meaning 'true/proper'

steps.m: to determine the measure or value of something

steps.n: Changes verb into noun meaning 'the act/result of measuring'
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: His true measure/worth (referring to Allah's worth)
steps.n: Added هِۦٓ which means 'His' (referring to Allah). This word is part of the phrase حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ meaning 'His true measure/worth'

Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. It makes the statement that follows it stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is truly powerful and mighty.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: Allah (the one true God being acted upon)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning with special merging rules for this unique word. The ه at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence. This is the proper name for the One True God in Islam.
Root letters ق-و-ي became قَوِي by adding vowels to make an adjective meaning 'strong'
Added shadda (ّ) on ي to show extra strength
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this describes a quality
Added لَ at start to emphasize that Allah is definitely this way
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Pattern فَعِيل adds intensity to make an emphatic adjective
The tanween (double damma) shows this is describing Allah's general attribute of being mighty and powerful
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it specific and the shadda emphasizes its uniqueness. The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to be pure, to select the best

steps.n: The root letters were joined with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. The و at the end turned into ا because it's at the end of the word.
Added اِ at the start and ط after ص to show he did the action for himself (like picking something special). The ى at the end shows it happened in the past.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The ِ before final ى shows ongoing action. This word refers to what Allah does.
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere else
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ (the angels)

steps.m: to control or have power over something

steps.n: Root letters م-ل-ك mean 'power/control' - adding fathas makes it mean 'one with power' (angel)
Added ا and ئ to make the word plural, showing there are many angels
Added ة at the end to complete the plural form
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific - these particular angels
Added ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from). Also added the long alif (ٰٓ) which is just a writing style.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Changes from singular 'rasool' to plural 'rusul' by changing the middle vowel from 'u' to 'u' and dropping the long 'oo' sound
Adds ًا to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَصْطَفِى (chooses/selects) in this ayah
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added fatha at the end to connect with the next word (الناس)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Root أنس meaning 'to be social' became نَاس meaning 'people' - like how humans are social beings who live together
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ن makes the word stronger/more emphatic
Added ٱل to show we're talking about specific people, not just any people
Added ِ at the end because this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a basic word meaning 'if/that' into a strong emphasis word. It's like adding an exclamation mark in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is All-Hearing and All-Seeing.
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters. The ل gets a shadda because two ل letters combine.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed). Also changed alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ) which is a spelling variation.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to hear'
Changed from 'heard' (past action) to 'All-Hearing' (permanent quality) by adding ي and changing vowels to make it a special name/quality of Allah
The double mark (tanween) at the end shows this is being used as a description, like saying 'Allah is All-Hearing' rather than just the word 'All-Hearing' by itself
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed vowels to show someone who sees perfectly and completely. This word works with سميعٌ to describe Allah in this ayah.
The dots and marks were removed from the letters, but the double damma (ٌ) at the end stays to show it's complete. Works with سميعٌ to tell us about Allah.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.m: he knows (what is مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word is connected to what follows it - Allah knows what is before and after them.
Word is already in its final form - a present tense verb meaning 'he knows'

steps.m: that which (object of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun meaning 'that which' or 'whatever'. In this ayah, it's the object of يَعْلَمُ (He knows), so it means 'He knows that which...'

steps.m: to be between or separate things
between (what is in front of them)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it into a word meaning 'between'. In this ayah, it's used to talk about what is in front of them (أَيْدِيهِمْ literally means 'their hands' but here means 'what is before them').
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple hands

steps.m: their hands (what is before them in أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show possession. In this ayah, it's used with خَلْفَهُمْ (what is behind them) to mean Allah knows everything before and after them.
This is a question word that asks about things or can mean 'whatever/that which' depending on context. In this ayah, it refers to 'what' is behind them.

steps.m: and what (connected to مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ - what is before them)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous one about 'what is before them'
Changed from root word to 'behind' by adding vowel marks, connects to word after it (هُمْ)
Adding هُمْ to show this refers to multiple people (them)
Basic directional particle showing movement or connection towards something. Here it connects with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show 'to Allah'
Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon by the word before it (إِلَى).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The تُ prefix shows someone/something is being returned rather than returning themselves
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'command' by adding vowel marks (fat-ha and sukoon)
Changed from singular to plural by adding و and changing the middle vowels to u-sounds (damma)

steps.m: the matters, the affairs (subject of تُرْجَعُ)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال and final damma to show this is the subject doing the action in تُرْجَعُ (are returned)
Adding damma to show it's in nominative case
Adding ها changes it to a calling/addressing word
Adds يٰٓ at the beginning to create an emphatic call or address
Adds a small line above the letter to make the calling sound longer and stronger
Starting with the basic word that points to something. Added ي to make it connect with other words.
Added ن to make it refer to many people instead of just one
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the ones' instead of just 'ones'

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned after)
steps.n: Added final vowel mark and emphasis on ذ. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) after it to mean 'those who believed'

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, believe in

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding alif makes it mean 'to have faith' rather than just 'to be safe'

steps.m: they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is part of يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ (O you who have believed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed from 'he bowed' to 'bow!' by adding u-sound at start and making it a command
Changed to plural command by adding وا and changing hamza to wasl

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground
he bowed down with forehead touching ground
steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he prostrated' to a command 'prostrate!' by adding اُ at start and changing the ending
Added وا at the end to make it a command for many people

steps.m: and bow down! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start and changed first letter to ٱ for connecting words
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to a command by adding 'u' at start and putting sukoon at end
Added plural ending to make it address multiple people
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect with previous command (ٱسْجُدُوا۟)
Root letters combined into a noun with shadda (double letter) to mean 'the one who nurtures'
Added كُمْ at the end to show the Lord belongs to all those being addressed (in this case, the believers mentioned in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he did' to 'do!' by dropping first letter and adding a command marker at start
Added plural masculine suffix وا to indicate multiple male addressees

steps.m: and do! (command to a group) (followed by ٱلْخَيْرَ - the good)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' at the start. This command is connected to other commands in the verse (bow, prostrate, worship) and is followed by 'the good' as its object.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to something good or beneficial
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon in the phrase 'do the good'
This is a special word that shows hope or possibility. It often comes before verbs to show what might happen.
Added a small mark (shadda) to show the 'L' sound is doubled/emphasized

steps.m: perhaps you all (expressing hope that you will succeed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to تُفْلِحُونَ (you will succeed) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and kasra on second root letter
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تُ
Added ونَ to show this refers to multiple people and implies future tense in this context

steps.m: to strive or struggle with maximum effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding alif shows interaction with others or intensity of action
Changed from past tense 'he struggled' to command form 'struggle!' by changing middle vowel to 'i' and end vowel to 'u'
Added وا to make it a command for a group
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it specifically shows location within 'the religion'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the preposition بِ (in/with). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Basic meaning of truth or reality
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he made true' in the past tense
Combined the repeated ق letters with a shadda to show emphasis. This word describes how the striving (جِهَادِ) should be done - in a true and proper way.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (to strive) into a noun meaning 'intense striving'
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end to show it belongs to or connects with حَقَّ (meaning 'true/right') that comes before it

steps.m: He (referring to Allah who named you Muslims)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun that emphasizes who did the action. In this ayah, it emphasizes that it was Allah Himself who named the believers Muslims.
This is the root meaning showing the basic idea of careful selection
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowel marks
Added Form VIII pattern (ا and ت) to show this is a special kind of choosing
Changed hamza to wasl form and added alif maksura
Added pronoun suffix to show who was chosen
This is a particle that can mean either 'what' (asking about something) or 'not' (negating something). Here it means 'not' because it comes before the verb جَعَلَ (he made/placed).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This و means 'and'.

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way
steps.n: Basic meaning of making or placing
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this verb connects to عَلَيْكُمْ (upon you) to show what was made/set.

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is affecting, covering, or positioned above something else. It's like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English.

steps.m: upon (ready to connect to a pronoun)
steps.n: The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to pronouns. This is like getting a word ready to show who it affects.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this affects 'you all' (when talking to a group). In this ayah, this word connects to شَهِيدًا (witness), showing the Prophet is a witness over all of you.
This is a basic preposition that connects two words by showing that one thing happens within or as part of another. Here it shows that the striving (جٰهِدُوا) happens in/for Allah's cause (ٱللَّهِ).

steps.m: to follow a way or path
steps.n: The root relates to following a way or path, which developed into meaning religion
Added vowels to make the noun form. The long ي shows this is a path or system you follow.
The shadda emphasizes the meaning, showing this is an established way of life

steps.m: the religion (the established way of life)
steps.n: Adding ٱل makes it refer to the specific religion being discussed
The ِ ending shows this word comes after the word فِى (in/concerning)

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects the word حَرَجٍ (difficulty) with what follows, showing there is no difficulty from/in the religion.
The root gives the idea of something being difficult or constrained
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
The tanween (two dots under) shows this is indefinite (any difficulty). It has this ending because it comes after the word مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah.

steps.m: to follow a path or way repeatedly
steps.n: Converting root into a noun pattern
Added shadda (doubled letter) and feminine ending to create a noun meaning a complete system or way of life

steps.m: religion, way of life (in accusative case)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word is in the accusative case

steps.m: the religion (as object of اتَّبِعُوا - follow)
steps.n: The letter م has a shaddah (doubled) to show connection with previous word, and the word is the object (thing being followed)
Added vowels to make the basic noun form of father
Added kasra and ي to show possession relationship
Added كُمْ to show the father belongs to multiple people being addressed

This is a proper noun of foreign origin (Hebrew: אַבְרָהָם/Avraham), so it doesn't follow regular Arabic root patterns. It refers to Prophet Abraham, the father figure mentioned as أَبِيكُمْ (your father) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: Abraham (as the object of مِلَّةَ - religion/way)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows this is the object of مِلَّةَ (religion/way), meaning 'the religion/way of Abraham'
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is the subject of the verb ٱجْتَبَىٰكُمْ (chose you).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense
The middle letter is doubled to add emphasis and make it transitive
Adding كُمُ suffix to indicate 'you all' as the object of the verb
The مُ prefix and vowel pattern creates an active participle - someone who does the action
The ين suffix creates regular masculine plural form
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: the ones who submit (to Allah) (object of سَمَّىٰكُمُ - He named you)
steps.n: Adding the accusative case ending shows this word is receiving the action of being named in the phrase 'He named you Muslims'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it's used with حَرَجٍ (difficulty) to show there is no difficulty coming from the religion.
Added vowel marks to create the basic noun form meaning 'front' or 'before'

steps.m: before (referring to مِن before this word in the ayah)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the main word in this part of the sentence. This word works with مِن before it to mean 'from before'
Basic preposition indicating location or containment
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Basic attention-getting sound used to point to things
The alif is replaced with a small alif above to show it's a quick sound
Added ذَ which means 'this' or 'that' to specify what to look at

steps.m: this (pointing to something in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show we're pointing to something masculine or neutral
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added يَ at start to show present/future tense and changed final vowel to ُ to show it's happening now

steps.m: so that he may be (connected to الرَّسُولُ شَهِيدًا - the messenger being a witness)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose and changed final vowel to َ to show it's dependent on something else. This explains why the messenger should be a witness.
Basic meaning of sending something
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda because it comes after ال
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb يَكُونَ (to be) in this ayah

steps.m: to witness or observe something directly
steps.n: Basic meaning of observing and giving testimony
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed from a past tense verb (he witnessed) to a noun describing someone who witnesses by adding ي

steps.m: a witness (object of لِيَكُونَ - so that he may be)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object of لِيَكُونَ (so that he may be). This connects to the next part where it says عَلَيْكُمْ (over you).
Basic preposition indicating position above or responsibility. In this ayah, it's used to show what has been placed upon people.
Changed the ending to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: upon you all (object of جَعَلَ meaning 'He placed')
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (He placed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'was' to present tense 'become' by adding تَ at the start - can mean either 'you become' or 'she becomes'
Changes from 'you/she becomes' to 'you all become' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: and you all may become (witnesses on people)
steps.n: Added conjunction prefix وَ to connect this verb with previous phrases. This verb introduces what these people will become - witnesses over mankind. The small circle after the word (۟) is a pronunciation mark that doesn't affect meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from 'he witnessed' to 'witness/testifier' by adding ي in middle - like changing 'help' to 'helper'
Makes the word plural by adding اء - like changing 'witness' to 'witnesses'

This is a basic preposition that shows something is on, upon or over something else. In this ayah, it connects 'witnesses' (شُهَدَآءَ) with 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسِ), showing that the believers will be witnesses over mankind.
The root transforms into the collective noun meaning 'people'
Adds alif after ن to create the basic word for 'people'
Added 'the' (ال) at the beginning, which caused the ن to be doubled in pronunciation

steps.m: of the people (after the word عَلَى meaning 'upon')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon)
Basic meaning of the three root letters
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at start to make causative (make something happen). The و changes to ا due to being between two vowels.
Changed from 'he established' to 'establish!' by making it a command. The long ا in the middle becomes ي when making commands.
Added وا to make it a command for a group
Added فَ at start to show this follows from previous statement

steps.m: to pray or connect with God
steps.n: Forms the noun meaning 'prayer'
Root letters of 'pray' got vowel marks (َ) to make it into the word 'prayer'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of prayer
Added long vowel marker (ٰ) to show the extended sound
Added ٱل (the) to make it a specific prayer + changed ending
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِيمُوا)
Added و meaning 'and'
Added long alif to make it mean 'giving' rather than just 'coming'
Changed middle vowel to make it passive - something is being given rather than someone giving
Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group

steps.m: to purify or make something grow
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of making something pure and helping it grow
Changed root letters into past tense 'he' verb by adding vowels
Changed the word into a noun meaning the act of purifying, which became the word for obligatory charity in Islam
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the first letter
Added the ending that shows this word is receiving the action of giving (from the verb وَءَاتُوا۟ meaning 'and give')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ت between first two root letters makes it mean 'to hold oneself firmly to something'
Adding و connects this word to what came before it, meaning 'and'
Adding وا makes it plural - now it means 'they' instead of 'he'
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but is a unique word that only refers to the One God.
The shadda shows that the ل is doubled for emphasis in this special word.
The small diagonal line under the last letter shows this means 'of Allah' or 'belonging to Allah'
The بِ at the beginning shows how something is done - in this case, holding fast to Allah.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun used here to emphasize that it is Allah who did these actions. It connects to the verbs اجْتَبَىٰكُمْ (chose you) and سَمَّىٰكُمُ (named you) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be close to or protect

steps.n: Added alif mark (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound
Added كُمْ at the end to show it's your protector (talking to many people)
Changes to a special verb used to praise something

steps.m: and how excellent! (connected to مَوْلَىٰكُمْ - your protector)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this praise to the previous statement about Allah being our protector (مَوْلَىٰكُمْ). It's like saying 'He is your protector, and what an excellent protector!'

steps.m: to protect or be close to

steps.n: Added مَ at the start and changed the ending to ى to make it mean 'the one who protects/helps' (like turning 'protect' into 'protector')

steps.m: the protector (subject of نِعْمَ - how excellent)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific protector. This word is being praised in the phrase 'how excellent is the protector'

steps.m: to be pleasant, to feel good

steps.n: Changes to a special verb used to praise something
The و connects this praise of Allah as Protector to the previous statement about Him being our مَوْلَى (Protector)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي and changed middle vowel to kasra to show someone who does this action intensively
Adds 'the' (ال) to show we're talking about a specific helper/supporter
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject of نِعْمَ (how excellent) in this ayah
